info.name: Akeyless API - Full Collection (v5)
info.description:
Complete Akeyless API collection: all 622 operations, generated from the official OpenAPI spec (akeylesslabs/akeyless-go, branch v5).

## Setup
Every request authenticates with a session token in the `{{token}}` variable. Populate it one of two ways:
- **Any auth method (SAML, OIDC, LDAP, cert, Kubernetes, API key):** obtain a token out of band and paste it into `{{token}}`. From the CLI: `akeyless auth` prints a token (t-...). Or copy one from the web console. Then set `base_url` and run any request.
- **API key only (convenience):** set `access_id` and `access_key`, then run **Auth & Auth Methods > auth**; its test script fills `{{token}}`.

Tokens expire after ~60 minutes; paste a fresh one or re-run `auth`.

## Base URL
- Public SaaS: `https://api.akeyless.io`
- Gateway: `https://<your-gateway-host>:8000/api/v2`

## Conventions
- Requests marked **[W]** are mutating (create/update/delete/set/rotate).
- Request bodies contain **required fields only**, as `<placeholders>`.
- Each request's **description documents every field** (required/optional, type, enum, default, and meaning) pulled from the spec.

## Auth model
Every command authenticates by carrying the session token in the JSON body field `token`. There is no Authorization header.

item.name: Auth & Auth Methods (62)
item.description: Authenticate (run `auth` first to populate {{token}}) and manage auth methods. Most work on public SaaS.

request.description:
## Authenticate to the service and returns a token to be used as a profile to execute the CLI without the need for re-authentication
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/auth

Required: none

Fields:
- `access-id` (optional, string): Access ID
- `access-key` (optional, string): Access key (relevant only for access-type=access_key)
- `access-type` (optional, string, default: access_key): Access Type (access_key/password/saml/ldap/k8s/azure_ad/oidc/aws_iam/universal_identity/jwt/gcp/cert/oci/kerberos)
- `account-id` (optional, string): Account id (relevant only for access-type=password where the email address is associated with more than one account)
- `admin-email` (optional, string): Email (relevant only for access-type=password)
- `admin-password` (optional, string): Password (relevant only for access-type=password)
- `azure-cloud` (optional, string, default: AzureCloud): Azure cloud environment to use. Values: AzureCloud (default), AzureUSGovernment, AzureChinaCloud.
- `cert-challenge` (optional, string): Certificate challenge encoded in base64. (relevant only for access-type=cert)
- `cert-data` (optional, string): Certificate data encoded in base64. Used if file was not provided. (relevant only for access-type=cert)
- `cloud-id` (optional, string): The cloud identity (relevant only for access-type=azure_ad,aws_iam,gcp)
- `debug` (optional, boolean)
- `disable-pafxfast` (optional, string): Disable the FAST negotiation in the Kerberos authentication method
- `gateway-spn` (optional, string): The service principal name of the gateway as registered in LDAP (i.e., HTTP/gateway)
- `gateway-url` (optional, string): Gateway URL relevant only for access-type=k8s/oauth2/saml/oidc
- `gcp-audience` (optional, string, default: akeyless.io): GCP JWT audience
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt` (optional, string): The Json Web Token (relevant only for access-type=jwt/oidc)
- `k8s-auth-config-name` (optional, string): The K8S Auth config name (relevant only for access-type=k8s)
- `k8s-service-account-token` (optional, string): The K8S service account token. (relevant only for access-type=k8s)
- `kerberos-token` (optional, string): KerberosToken represents a Kerberos token generated for the gateway SPN (Service Principal Name).
- `kerberos-username` (optional, string): TThe username for the entry within the keytab to authenticate via Kerberos
- `key-data` (optional, string): Private key data encoded in base64. Used if file was not provided.(relevant only for access-type=cert)
- `keytab-data` (optional, string): Base64-encoded content of a valid keytab file, containing the service account's entry.
- `krb5-conf-data` (optional, string): Base64-encoded content of a valid krb5.conf file, specifying the settings and parameters required for Kerberos authentication.
- `ldap-password` (optional, string): LDAP password (relevant only for access-type=ldap)
- `oci-auth-type` (optional, string, default: apikey): The type of the OCI configuration to use [instance/apikey/resource] (relevant only for access-type=oci)
- `oci-group-ocid` (optional, array): A list of Oracle Cloud IDs groups (relevant only for access-type=oci)
- `otp` (optional, string)
- `signed-cert-challenge` (optional, string): Signed certificate challenge encoded in base64. (relevant only for access-type=cert)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal_identity token (relevant only for access-type=universal_identity)
- `use-remote-browser` (optional, boolean): Returns a link to complete the authentication remotely (relevant only for access-type=saml/oidc)
- `username` (optional, string): LDAP username (relevant only for access-type=ldap)

---
This request covers API-key auth (`access-type: api_key`). Set
`access_id` and `access_key`, run it, and its test script stores the
token in `{{token}}`.

For any other auth method (SAML, OIDC, LDAP, cert, Kubernetes, etc.) do
NOT use this request. Obtain a token out of band and set the `{{token}}`
variable directly: run `akeyless auth` in the CLI, or copy a token from
the Akeyless web console. To drive a non-api-key method through this
request instead, change `access-type` and supply that method's fields
(for example `jwt` for OIDC/JWT, `cert-data`/`key-data` for cert,
`k8s-auth-config-name` for Kubernetes).

## Create a new API Key Auth Method in the account
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth-method-create-api-key`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodcreateapikey

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Create a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using AWS IAM credentials
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth-method-create-aws-iam`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodcreateawsiam

Required: bound-aws-account-id, name

Fields:
- `bound-aws-account-id` (**required**, array): A list of AWS account-IDs that the access is restricted to
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-arn` (optional, array): A list of full arns that the access is restricted to
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-resource-id` (optional, array): A list of full resource ids that the access is restricted to
- `bound-role-id` (optional, array): A list of full role ids that the access is restricted to
- `bound-role-name` (optional, array): A list of full role-name that the access is restricted to
- `bound-user-id` (optional, array): A list of full user ids that the access is restricted to
- `bound-user-name` (optional, array): A list of full user-name that the access is restricted to
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `sts-url` (optional, string, default: https://sts.amazonaws.com): sts URL
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `unique-identifier` (optional, string): A unique identifier (ID) value which is a "sub claim" name that contains details uniquely identifying that resource. This "sub claim" is used to distinguish between different identities.

## Create a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using Azure Active Directory credentials
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth-method-create-azure-ad`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodcreateazuread

Required: bound-tenant-id, name

Fields:
- `bound-tenant-id` (**required**, string): The Azure tenant id that the access is restricted to
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `audience` (optional, string, default: https://management.azure.com/): Deprecated (Deprecated) The audience in the JWT
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-group-id` (optional, array): A list of group ids that the access is restricted to
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-providers` (optional, array): A list of resource providers that the access is restricted to (e.g, Microsoft.Compute, Microsoft.ManagedIdentity, etc)
- `bound-resource-id` (optional, array): A list of full resource ids that the access is restricted to
- `bound-resource-names` (optional, array): A list of resource names that the access is restricted to (e.g, a virtual machine name, scale set name, etc).
- `bound-resource-types` (optional, array): A list of resource types that the access is restricted to (e.g, virtualMachines, userAssignedIdentities, etc)
- `bound-rg-id` (optional, array): A list of resource groups that the access is restricted to
- `bound-spid` (optional, array): A list of service principal IDs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-sub-id` (optional, array): A list of subscription ids that the access is restricted to
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `issuer` (optional, string, default: https://sts.windows.net/---bound_tenant_id---): Issuer URL
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwks-uri` (optional, string, default: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/discovery/keys): The URL to the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) that containing the public keys that should be used to verify any JSON Web Token (JWT) issued by the authorization server.
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `unique-identifier` (optional, string): A unique identifier (ID) value which is a "sub claim" name that contains details uniquely identifying that resource. This "sub claim" is used to distinguish between different identities.

## Create a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using a client certificate.
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth-method-create-cert`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodcreatecert

Required: name, unique-identifier

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `unique-identifier` (**required**, string): A unique identifier (ID) value should be configured, such as common_name or organizational_unit Whenever a user logs in with a token, these authentication types issue a "sub claim" that contains details uniquely identifying that user. This sub claim includes a key containing the ID value that you configured, and is used to distinguish between different users from within the same organization.
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `allowed-cors` (optional, string): Comma separated list of allowed CORS domains to be validated as part of the authentication flow.
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-common-names` (optional, array): A list of names. At least one must exist in the Common Name. Supports globbing.
- `bound-dns-sans` (optional, array): A list of DNS names. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `bound-email-sans` (optional, array): A list of Email Addresses. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `bound-extensions` (optional, array): A list of extensions formatted as "oid:value". Expects the extension value to be some type of ASN1 encoded string. All values much match. Supports globbing on "value".
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-organizational-units` (optional, array): A list of Organizational Units names. At least one must exist in the OU field.
- `bound-uri-sans` (optional, array): A list of URIs. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `certificate-data` (optional, string): The certificate data in base64, if no file was provided
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `require-crl-dp` (optional, boolean): Require certificate CRL distribution points (CDP) and enforce CRL validation during authentication.
- `revoked-cert-ids` (optional, array): A list of revoked cert ids
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Create a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using an email address
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth-method-create-email`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodcreateemail

Required: email, name

Fields:
- `email` (**required**, string): An email address to be invited to have access
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `enable-mfa` (optional, string): Enable MFA for this authentication method [True / False]
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `mfa-type` (optional, string, default: email): Enable two-factor-authentication via [email/auth app]
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Create a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using GCP IAM Service Account credentials or GCE instance credentials
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth-method-create-gcp`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodcreategcp

Required: audience, name, type

Fields:
- `audience` (**required**, string, default: akeyless.io): The audience to verify in the JWT received by the client
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `type` (**required**, string): Type of the GCP Access Rules
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-labels` (optional, array): A comma-separated list of GCP labels formatted as "key:value" strings that must be set on authorized GCE instances. TODO: Because GCP labels are not currently ACL'd ....
- `bound-projects` (optional, array): === Human and Machine authentication section === Array of GCP project IDs. Only entities belonging to any of the provided projects can authenticate.
- `bound-regions` (optional, array): List of regions that a GCE instance must belong to in order to be authenticated. TODO: If bound_instance_groups is provided, it is assumed to be a regional group and the group must belong to this region. If bound_zones are provided, this attribute is ignored.
- `bound-service-accounts` (optional, array): List of service accounts the service account must be part of in order to be authenticated.
- `bound-zones` (optional, array): === Machine authentication section === List of zones that a GCE instance must belong to in order to be authenticated. TODO: If bound_instance_groups is provided, it is assumed to be a zonal group and the group must belong to this zone.
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `service-account-creds-data` (optional, string): ServiceAccount credentials data instead of giving a file path, base64 encoded
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `unique-identifier` (optional, string): A unique identifier (ID) value which is a "sub claim" name that contains details uniquely identifying that resource. This "sub claim" is used to distinguish between different identities.

## Create a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using Kubernetes
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth-method-create-k8s`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodcreatek8s

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `audience` (optional, string): The audience in the Kubernetes JWT that the access is restricted to
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-namespaces` (optional, array): A list of namespaces that the access is restricted to
- `bound-pod-names` (optional, array): A list of pod names that the access is restricted to
- `bound-sa-names` (optional, array): A list of service account names that the access is restricted to
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gen-key` (optional, string, default: true): Automatically generate key-pair for K8S configuration. If set to false, a public key needs to be provided [true/false]
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `public-key` (optional, string): Base64-encoded or PEM formatted public key data for K8S authentication method is required [RSA2048]
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Create a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using LDAP
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth-method-create-ldap`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodcreateldap

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gen-key` (optional, string, default: true): Automatically generate key-pair for LDAP configuration. If set to false, a public key needs to be provided [true/false]
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `public-key-data` (optional, string): A public key generated for LDAP authentication method on Akeyless in base64 or PEM format [RSA2048]
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `unique-identifier` (optional, string, default: users): A unique identifier (ID) value should be configured for OAuth2, LDAP and SAML authentication method types and is usually a value such as the email, username, or upn for example. Whenever a user logs in with a token, these authentication types issue a "sub claim" that contains details uniquely identifying that user. This sub claim includes a key containing the ID value that you configured, and is used to distinguish between different users from within the same organization.

## Update a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using a client certificate.

`POST /auth-method-update-cert`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodupdatecert

Required: name, unique-identifier

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `unique-identifier` (**required**, string): A unique identifier (ID) value should be configured, such as common_name or organizational_unit Whenever a user logs in with a token, these authentication types issue a "sub claim" that contains details uniquely identifying that user. This sub claim includes a key containing the ID value that you configured, and is used to distinguish between different users from within the same organization.
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `allowed-cors` (optional, string): Comma separated list of allowed CORS domains to be validated as part of the authentication flow.
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-common-names` (optional, array): A list of names. At least one must exist in the Common Name. Supports globbing.
- `bound-dns-sans` (optional, array): A list of DNS names. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `bound-email-sans` (optional, array): A list of Email Addresses. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `bound-extensions` (optional, array): A list of extensions formatted as "oid:value". Expects the extension value to be some type of ASN1 encoded string. All values much match. Supports globbing on "value".
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-organizational-units` (optional, array): A list of Organizational Units names. At least one must exist in the OU field.
- `bound-uri-sans` (optional, array): A list of URIs. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `certificate-data` (optional, string): The certificate data in base64, if no file was provided
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `new-name` (optional, string): Auth Method new name
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `require-crl-dp` (optional, boolean): Require certificate CRL distribution points (CDP) and enforce CRL validation during authentication.
- `revoked-cert-ids` (optional, array): A list of revoked cert ids

## Update a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using GCP IAM Service Account credentials or GCE instance credentials
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /auth-method-update-gcp`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/authmethodupdategcp

Required: audience, name, type

Fields:
- `audience` (**required**, string, default: akeyless.io): The audience to verify in the JWT received by the client
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `type` (**required**, string): Type of the GCP Access Rules
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-labels` (optional, array): A comma-separated list of GCP labels formatted as "key:value" strings that must be set on authorized GCE instances. TODO: Because GCP labels are not currently ACL'd ....
- `bound-projects` (optional, array): === Human and Machine authentication section === Array of GCP project IDs. Only entities belonging to any of the provided projects can authenticate.
- `bound-regions` (optional, array): List of regions that a GCE instance must belong to in order to be authenticated. TODO: If bound_instance_groups is provided, it is assumed to be a regional group and the group must belong to this region. If bound_zones are provided, this attribute is ignored.
- `bound-service-accounts` (optional, array): List of service accounts the service account must be part of in order to be authenticated.
- `bound-zones` (optional, array): === Machine authentication section === List of zones that a GCE instance must belong to in order to be authenticated. TODO: If bound_instance_groups is provided, it is assumed to be a zonal group and the group must belong to this zone.
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `new-name` (optional, string): Auth Method new name
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `service-account-creds-data` (optional, string): ServiceAccount credentials data instead of giving a file path, base64 encoded
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `unique-identifier` (optional, string): A unique identifier (ID) value which is a "sub claim" name that contains details uniquely identifying that resource. This "sub claim" is used to distinguish between different identities.

## Create a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using a client certificate.
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /auth-method-create-cert._

`POST /create-auth-method-cert`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createauthmethodcert

Required: name, unique-identifier

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `unique-identifier` (**required**, string): A unique identifier (ID) value should be configured, such as common_name or organizational_unit Whenever a user logs in with a token, these authentication types issue a "sub claim" that contains details uniquely identifying that user. This sub claim includes a key containing the ID value that you configured, and is used to distinguish between different users from within the same organization.
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `allowed-cors` (optional, string): Comma separated list of allowed CORS domains to be validated as part of the authentication flow.
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-common-names` (optional, array): A list of names. At least one must exist in the Common Name. Supports globbing.
- `bound-dns-sans` (optional, array): A list of DNS names. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `bound-email-sans` (optional, array): A list of Email Addresses. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `bound-extensions` (optional, array): A list of extensions formatted as "oid:value". Expects the extension value to be some type of ASN1 encoded string. All values much match. Supports globbing on "value".
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-organizational-units` (optional, array): A list of Organizational Units names. At least one must exist in the OU field.
- `bound-uri-sans` (optional, array): A list of URIs. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `certificate-data` (optional, string): The certificate data in base64, if no file was provided
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `require-crl-dp` (optional, boolean): Require certificate CRL distribution points (CDP) and enforce CRL validation during authentication.
- `revoked-cert-ids` (optional, array): A list of revoked cert ids
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `client-id` (optional, string): Client ID
- `client-secret` (optional, string): Client Secret
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `issuer` (optional, string): Issuer URL
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `required-scopes` (optional, array): RequiredScopes is a list of required scopes that the oidc method will request from the oidc provider and the user must approve
- `required-scopes-prefix` (optional, string): RequiredScopesPrefix is a a prefix to add to all required-scopes when requesting them from the oidc server (for example, azures' Application ID URI)
- `subclaims-delimiters` (optional, array): A list of additional sub claims delimiters (relevant only for SAML, OIDC, OAuth2/JWT)
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Update a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using a client certificate.
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /auth-method-update-cert._

`POST /update-auth-method-cert`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/updateauthmethodcert

Required: name, unique-identifier

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `unique-identifier` (**required**, string): A unique identifier (ID) value should be configured, such as common_name or organizational_unit Whenever a user logs in with a token, these authentication types issue a "sub claim" that contains details uniquely identifying that user. This sub claim includes a key containing the ID value that you configured, and is used to distinguish between different users from within the same organization.
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `allowed-cors` (optional, string): Comma separated list of allowed CORS domains to be validated as part of the authentication flow.
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-common-names` (optional, array): A list of names. At least one must exist in the Common Name. Supports globbing.
- `bound-dns-sans` (optional, array): A list of DNS names. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `bound-email-sans` (optional, array): A list of Email Addresses. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `bound-extensions` (optional, array): A list of extensions formatted as "oid:value". Expects the extension value to be some type of ASN1 encoded string. All values much match. Supports globbing on "value".
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `bound-organizational-units` (optional, array): A list of Organizational Units names. At least one must exist in the OU field.
- `bound-uri-sans` (optional, array): A list of URIs. At least one must exist in the SANs. Supports globbing.
- `certificate-data` (optional, string): The certificate data in base64, if no file was provided
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `new-name` (optional, string): Auth Method new name
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `require-crl-dp` (optional, boolean): Require certificate CRL distribution points (CDP) and enforce CRL validation during authentication.
- `revoked-cert-ids` (optional, array): A list of revoked cert ids
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to

- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL

- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `service-account-creds-data` (optional, string): ServiceAccount credentials data instead of giving a file path, base64 encoded
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `unique-identifier` (optional, string): A unique identifier (ID) value which is a "sub claim" name that contains details uniquely identifying that resource. This "sub claim" is used to distinguish between different identities.

- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `new-name` (optional, string): Auth Method new name
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `public-key-data` (optional, string): A public key generated for LDAP authentication method on Akeyless in base64 or PEM format [RSA2048]
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `unique-identifier` (optional, string, default: users): A unique identifier (ID) value should be configured for OAuth2, LDAP and SAML authentication method types and is usually a value such as the email, username, or upn for example. Whenever a user logs in with a token, these authentication types issue a "sub claim" that contains details uniquely identifying that user. This sub claim includes a key containing the ID value that you configured, and is used to distinguish between different users from within the same organization.

## Update a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using OAuth2
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /auth-method-update-oauth2._

`POST /update-auth-method-oauth2`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/updateauthmethodoauth2

Required: name, unique-identifier

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `unique-identifier` (**required**, string): A unique identifier (ID) value should be configured for OAuth2, LDAP and SAML authentication method types and is usually a value such as the email, username, or upn for example. Whenever a user logs in with a token, these authentication types issue a "sub claim" that contains details uniquely identifying that user. This sub claim includes a key containing the ID value that you configured, and is used to distinguish between different users from within the same organization.
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `audience` (optional, string): The audience in the JWT
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-client-ids` (optional, array): The clients ids that the access is restricted to
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `cert` (optional, string): CertificateFile Path to a file that contain the certificate in a PEM format.
- `cert-file-data` (optional, string): CertificateFileData PEM Certificate in a Base64 format.
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gateway-url` (optional, string): Akeyless Gateway URL (Configuration Management port). Relevant only when the jwks-uri is accessible only from the gateway.
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `issuer` (optional, string): Issuer URL

- `jwks-uri` (optional, string): The URL to the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) that containing the public keys that should be used to verify any JSON Web Token (JWT) issued by the authorization server.
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `new-name` (optional, string): Auth Method new name
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `subclaims-delimiters` (optional, array): A list of additional sub claims delimiters (relevant only for SAML, OIDC, OAuth2/JWT)
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Update a new Auth Method that will be able to authenticate using OIDC
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /auth-method-update-oidc._

`POST /update-auth-method-oidc`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/updateauthmethodoidc

Required: name, unique-identifier

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Auth Method name
- `unique-identifier` (**required**, string): A unique identifier (ID) value should be configured for OIDC, OAuth2, LDAP and SAML authentication method types and is usually a value such as the email, username, or upn for example. Whenever a user logs in with a token, these authentication types issue a "sub claim" that contains details uniquely identifying that user. This sub claim includes a key containing the ID value that you configured, and is used to distinguish between different users from within the same organization.
- `access-expires` (optional, integer, default: 0): Access expiration date in Unix timestamp (select 0 for access without expiry date)
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): limit the auth method usage for specific client types [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension]
- `allowed-redirect-uri` (optional, array): Allowed redirect URIs after the authentication
- `audience` (optional, string): Audience claim to be used as part of the authentication flow. In case set, it must match the one configured on the Identity Provider's Application
- `audit-logs-claims` (optional, array): Subclaims to include in audit logs, e.g "--audit-logs-claims email --audit-logs-claims username"
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the IPs that the access is restricted to
- `client-id` (optional, string): Client ID
- `client-secret` (optional, string): Client Secret
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Auth Method description
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the auth method would you like to be notified.
- `force-sub-claims` (optional, boolean): if true: enforce role-association must include sub claims
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A CIDR whitelist with the GW IPs that the access is restricted to
- `issuer` (optional, string): Issuer URL
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl` (optional, integer, default: 0): Jwt TTL
- `new-name` (optional, string): Auth Method new name
- `product-type` (optional, array): Choose the relevant product type for the auth method [sm, sra, pm, dp, ca]
- `required-scopes` (optional, array): RequiredScopes is a list of required scopes that the oidc method will request from the oidc provider and the user must approve
- `required-scopes-prefix` (optional, string): RequiredScopesPrefix is a a prefix to add to all required-scopes when requesting them from the oidc server (for example, azures' Application ID URI)
- `subclaims-delimiters` (optional, array): A list of additional sub claims delimiters (relevant only for SAML, OIDC, OAuth2/JWT)

- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
