item.name: Secrets & Items (17)
item.description: Static secrets and generic item operations. Work on public SaaS.

request.description:
## Creates a new static secret item
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /create-secret`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createsecret

Required: name, value

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Secret name
- `value` (**required**, string): The secret value (relevant only for type 'generic')
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `change-event` (optional, string): Trigger an event when a secret value changed [true/false] (Relevant only for Static Secret)
- `custom-field` (optional, object): For Password Management use, additional fields
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `format` (optional, string, default: text): Secret format [text/json/key-value] (relevant only for type 'generic')
- `inject-url` (optional, array): For Password Management use, reflect the website context
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input)
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `lock-during-sra-session` (optional, string): Lock this secret for read/update while an SRA session is active
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `metadata` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `multiline_value` (optional, boolean): The provided value is a multiline value (separated by '\n')
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `password` (optional, string): For Password Management use, additional fields
- `protection_key` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `secure-access-bastion-issuer` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-certificate-issuer
- `secure-access-certificate-issuer` (optional, string): Path to the SSH Certificate Issuer for your Akeyless Secure Access
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `secure-access-gateway` (optional, string)
- `secure-access-host` (optional, array): Target servers for connections (In case of Linked Target association, host(s) will inherit Linked Target hosts - Relevant only for Dynamic Secrets/producers)
- `secure-access-rdp-user` (optional, string): Remote Desktop Username
- `secure-access-ssh-creds` (optional, string): Static-Secret values contains SSH Credentials, either Private Key or Password [password/private-key]
- `secure-access-ssh-user` (optional, string): Override the SSH username as indicated in SSH Certificate Issuer
- `secure-access-url` (optional, string): Destination URL to inject secrets
- `secure-access-web-browsing` (optional, boolean, default: False): Secure browser via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `secure-access-web-proxy` (optional, boolean, default: False): Web-Proxy via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `type` (optional, string, default: generic): The secret sub type [generic/password]
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `username` (optional, string): For Password Management use

## Delete an item or an item version
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /delete-item`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/deleteitem

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Item name
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `delete-immediately` (optional, boolean, default: False): When delete-in-days=-1, must be set
- `delete-in-days` (optional, integer, default: 7): The number of days to wait before deleting the item (relevant for keys only)
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `version` (optional, integer, default: -1): The specific version you want to delete - 0=last version, -1=entire item with all versions (default)

## Delete multiple items from a given path
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /delete-items`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/deleteitems

Required: none

Fields:
- `item` (optional, array): A list of items to delete, To specify multiple items use argument multiple times
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `path` (optional, string): Path to delete the items from
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Get the item details
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /describe-item`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/describeitem

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Item name
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `bastion-details` (optional, boolean, default: False): Indicate if the item should return with ztb cluster details (url, etc)
- `der-certificate-format` (optional, boolean, default: False): The certificate will be displayed in DER format
- `display-id` (optional, string): The display id of the item
- `gateway-details` (optional, boolean, default: False): Indicate if the item should return with clusters details (url, etc)
- `item-custom-fields-details` (optional, boolean, default: False): Include all item custom fields details
- `item-id` (optional, integer): Item id of the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `services-details` (optional, boolean, default: False): Include all associated services details
- `show-versions` (optional, boolean, default: False): Include all item versions in reply
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Get static secret value
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /get-secret-value`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/getsecretvalue

Required: names

Fields:
- `names` (**required**, array): Secret name
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `ignore-cache` (optional, string, default: false): Retrieve the Secret value without checking the Gateway's cache [true/false]. This flag is only relevant when using the RestAPI
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `pretty-print` (optional, boolean): Print the secret value with json-pretty-print (not relevent to SDK)
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `version` (optional, integer): Secret version, if negative value N is provided the last N versions will return (maximum 20)

## List items
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /list-items`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/listitems

Required: none

Fields:
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `advanced-filter` (optional, string): Filter by item name/username/website or part of it
- `ara-only` (optional, boolean, default: False): Filter by items with ARA functionality enabled
- `auto-pagination` (optional, string, default: enabled): Retrieve all items using pagination, when disabled retrieving only first 1000 items
- `current-folder` (optional, boolean, default: False): List only items in the current folder (excludes subfolders)
- `filter` (optional, string): Filter by item name or part of it
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `minimal-view` (optional, boolean): Show only basic information of the items
- `modified-after` (optional, integer): List only secrets modified after specified date (in unix time)
- `pagination-token` (optional, string): Next page reference
- `path` (optional, string): Path to folder
- `sra-only` (optional, boolean, default: False): Filter by items with SRA functionality enabled
- `sub-types` (optional, array)
- `tag` (optional, string): Filter by item tag
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `type` (optional, array): The item types list of the requested items. In case it is empty, all types of items will be returned. options: [key, static-secret, dynamic-secret, classic-key]
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## List shared items
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /list-shared-items`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/listshareditems

Required: none

Fields:
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Lock a static secret item
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /lock-item`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/lockitem

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Item name
- `actions` (optional, string): Comma-separated blocked actions (read, update)
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `lock-ttl` (optional, integer): Lock TTL in minutes
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Rollback secret to older version
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /rollback-secret`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/rollbacksecret

Required: name, old-version

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Secret name
- `old-version` (**required**, integer): Old secret version to rollback to
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Rotates an OIDC client secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /rotate-oidc-client-secret`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/rotateoidcclientsecret

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): OIDC application name
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Set an item's state (Enabled, Disabled)
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /set-item-state`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/setitemstate

Required: desired-state, name

Fields:
- `desired-state` (**required**, string): Desired item state (Enabled, Disabled)
- `name` (**required**, string): Current item name
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `version` (optional, integer, default: 0): The specific version you want to update: 0=item level state (default) (relevant only for keys)

## Sharing item operation [start sharing / stop sharing / sharing describe]
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /share-item`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/shareitem

Required: action, item-name

Fields:
- `action` (**required**, string): Action to be performed on the item [start/stop/describe]
- `item-name` (**required**, string): Item name
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `emails` (optional, array): List of emails to start/stop sharing the secret with
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `share-type` (optional, string, default: email): Share type [email/token]
- `shared-token-id` (optional, array): Shared token ids in order to stop sharing a secret
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `ttl` (optional, integer): TTL of the Availability of the shared secret in seconds
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `view-once` (optional, boolean, default: False): ViewOnlyOnce Shared secrets can only be viewed once [true/false]

## Delete static secret sync
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /static-secret-delete-sync`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/staticsecretdeletesync

Required: name, usc-name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Static secret name
- `usc-name` (**required**, string): Universal Secret Connector name
- `delete-from-usc` (optional, boolean, default: False): Delete the secret from the remote target USC as well
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `remote-secret-name` (optional, string): Remote Secret Name to disambiguate when multiple syncs exist under the same USC
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Sync static secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /static-secret-sync`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/staticsecretsync

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Static secret name
- `delete-remote` (optional, boolean): Delete the secret from remote secret manager (for association create/update)
- `filter-secret-value` (optional, string): JQ expression to filter or transform the secret value
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `namespace` (optional, string): Vault namespace, releavnt only for Hashicorp Vault Target
- `remote-secret-name` (optional, string): Remote Secret Name that will be synced on the remote endpoint
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `usc-name` (optional, string): Universal Secret Connector name, If not provided all attached USC's will be synced

## Unlock a static secret item
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /unlock-item`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/unlockitem

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Item name
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Update item name and metadata
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /update-item`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/updateitem

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Current item name
- `ProviderType` (optional, string)
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `add-tag` (optional, array): List of the new tags that will be attached to this item
- `cert-file-data` (optional, string): PEM Certificate in a Base64 format. Used for updating RSA keys' certificates.
- `certificate-format` (optional, string)
- `change-event` (optional, string): Trigger an event when a secret value changed [true/false] (Relevant only for Static Secret)
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string, default: default_metadata): Description of the object
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the certificate would you like to be notified.
- `gcp-sm-regions` (optional, string): GCP Secret Manager regions to query for regional secrets (comma-separated, e.g., us-east1,us-west1). Max 12 regions. USC with GCP targets only.
- `host-provider` (optional, string): Host provider type [explicit/target], Default Host provider is explicit, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access of ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `lock-during-sra-session` (optional, string): Lock this secret for read/update while an SRA session is active
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `new-metadata` (optional, string, default: default_metadata): Deprecated - use description
- `new-name` (optional, string): New item name
- `rm-tag` (optional, array): List of the existent tags that will be removed from this item
- `rotate-after-disconnect` (optional, string): StringOrBool accepts JSON strings, booleans, and numbers for backward compatibility with older SDK versions that send boolean values for rotate-after-disconnect.
- `secure-access-add-host` (optional, array): List of the new hosts that will be attached to SRA servers host
- `secure-access-allow-external-user` (optional, string): Allow providing external user for a domain users [true/false]
- `secure-access-allow-port-forwading` (optional, boolean): Enable Port forwarding while using CLI access (relevant only for EKS/GKE/K8s Dynamic-Secret)
- `secure-access-api` (optional, string): Bastion's SSH control API endpoint. E.g. https://my.sra-server:9900 (relevant only for ssh cert issuer)
- `secure-access-aws-account-id` (optional, string): The AWS account id (relevant only for aws)
- `secure-access-aws-native-cli` (optional, boolean): The AWS native cli (relevant only for aws)
- `secure-access-aws-region` (optional, string): The AWS region (relevant only for aws)
- `secure-access-bastion-api` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-api
- `secure-access-bastion-issuer` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-certificate-issuer
- `secure-access-bastion-ssh` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-ssh
- `secure-access-certificate-issuer` (optional, string): Path to the SSH Certificate Issuer for your Akeyless Secure Access
- `secure-access-cluster-endpoint` (optional, string): The K8s cluster endpoint URL (relevant only for EKS/GKE/K8s Dynamic-Secret)
- `secure-access-dashboard-url` (optional, string): The K8s dashboard url (relevant only for k8s)
- `secure-access-db-name` (optional, string): The DB name (relevant only for DB Dynamic-Secret)
- `secure-access-db-schema` (optional, string): The DB schema (relevant only for DB Dynamic-Secret)
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `secure-access-gateway` (optional, string)
- `secure-access-host` (optional, array): Target servers for connections (In case of Linked Target association, host(s) will inherit Linked Target hosts - Relevant only for Dynamic Secrets/producers)
- `secure-access-rd-gateway-server` (optional, string): RD Gateway server (relevant only for rdp)
- `secure-access-rdp-domain` (optional, string): Required when the Dynamic Secret is used for a domain user (relevant only for RDP Dynamic-Secret)
- `secure-access-rdp-user` (optional, string): Override the RDP Domain username
- `secure-access-rm-host` (optional, array): List of the existent hosts that will be removed from SRA servers host
- `secure-access-ssh` (optional, string): Bastion's SSH server. E.g. my.sra-server:22 (relevant only for ssh cert issuer)
- `secure-access-ssh-creds` (optional, string): Secret values contains SSH Credentials, either Private Key or Password [password/private-key] (relevant only for Static-Secret or Rotated-secret)
- `secure-access-ssh-creds-user` (optional, string): SSH username to connect to target server, must be in 'Allowed Users' list (relevant only for ssh cert issuer)
- `secure-access-url` (optional, string): Destination URL to inject secrets
- `secure-access-use-internal-bastion` (optional, boolean): Deprecated. Use secure-access-use-internal-ssh-access
- `secure-access-use-internal-ssh-access` (optional, boolean): Use internal SSH Access
- `secure-access-web-browsing` (optional, boolean, default: False): Secure browser via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `secure-access-web-proxy` (optional, boolean, default: False): Web-Proxy via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `usc-tags` (optional, string): Comma-separated list of tags to apply to all secrets created/synced on the remote USC  USC items only.
- `use-tags-as-filter` (optional, string): Whether to filter the USC secret list using the specified usc-tags [true/false]  USC items only.

## Update static secret value
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /update-secret-val`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/updatesecretval

Required: name, value

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Secret name
- `value` (**required**, string): The secret value (relevant only for type 'generic')
- `accessibility` (optional, string, default: regular): for personal password manager
- `custom-field` (optional, object): For Password Management use, additional fields
- `format` (optional, string, default: text): Secret format [text/json/key-value] (relevant only for type 'generic')
- `inject-url` (optional, array): For Password Management use, reflect the website context
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `keep-prev-version` (optional, string): Whether to keep previous version [true/false]. If not set, use default according to account settings
- `key` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `last-version` (optional, integer): The last version number before the update
- `multiline` (optional, boolean): The provided value is a multiline value (separated by '\n')
- `new-version` (optional, boolean): Deprecated
- `password` (optional, string): For Password Management use, additional fields
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `username` (optional, string): For Password Management use

item.name: Roles & RBAC (10)
item.description: Roles and access rules. Work on public SaaS.

request.description:
## Create an association between role and auth method
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /assoc-role-am`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/assoc-role-am

Required: am-name, role-name

Fields:
- `am-name` (**required**, string): The auth method to associate
- `role-name` (**required**, string): The role to associate
- `case-sensitive` (optional, string, default: true): Treat sub claims as case-sensitive [true/false]
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `sub-claims` (optional, object): key/val of sub claims, e.g group=admins,developers
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Creates a new role
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /create-role`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createrole

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Role name
- `analytics-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view analytics. Currently only 'none', 'own', 'all' values are supported, allowing associated auth methods to view reports produced by the same auth methods.
- `ara-reports-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view Agentic Runtime Authority Dashboard. Currently only 'none', 'scoped', 'all' values are supported.
- `audit-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view audit logs. Currently only 'none', 'own', 'scoped' and 'all' values are supported, allowing associated auth methods to view audit logs produced by the same auth methods.
- `comment` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `event-center-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view Event Center. Currently only 'none', 'scoped' and 'all' values are supported
- `event-forwarders-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to manage Event Forwarders. Currently only 'none' and 'all' values are supported.
- `event-forwarders-name` (optional, array): Allow this role to manage the following Event Forwarders.
- `gw-analytics-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view gw analytics. Currently only 'none', 'scoped', 'all' values are supported, allowing associated auth methods to view reports produced by the same auth methods.
- `isi-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to access Identity & Secrets Intelligence. Currently only 'none', 'scoped' and 'all' values are supported.
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `reverse-rbac-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view Reverse RBAC. Supported values: 'scoped', 'all'.
- `sra-reports-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view SRA Clusters. Currently only 'none', 'scoped', 'all' values are supported.
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `usage-reports-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view Usage Report. Currently only 'none' and 'all' values are supported.

## Delete a role
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /delete-role`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/deleterole

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Role name
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Delete a rule from a role
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /delete-role-rule`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/deleterolerule

Required: path, role-name

Fields:
- `path` (**required**, string): The path the rule refers to
- `role-name` (**required**, string): The role name to be updated
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `rule-type` (optional, string, default: item-rule): item-rule, role-rule, auth-method-rule, search-rule, reports-rule, gw-reports-rule, sra-reports-rule, ara-reports-rule, sra-rule, ara-rule, usage-reports-rule, event-rule, event-forwarder-rule, reverse-rbac-rule
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Delete multiple roles from a given path
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /delete-roles`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/deleteroles

Required: path

Fields:
- `path` (**required**, string): Path to delete the auth methods from
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Describe role association details
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /describe-role-am-assoc`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/describeassoc

Required: assoc-id

Fields:
- `assoc-id` (**required**, string): The association id
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Get role details
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /get-role`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/getrole

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Role name
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## List of all roles in the account
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /list-roles`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/listroles

Required: none

Fields:
- `filter` (optional, string): Filter by item name or part of it
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `pagination-token` (optional, string): Next page reference
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Set a rule to a role
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /set-role-rule`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/setrolerule

Required: capability, path, role-name

Fields:
- `capability` (**required**, array): List of the approved/denied capabilities in the path options: [read, create, update, delete, list, deny]
- `path` (**required**, string): The path the rule refers to
- `role-name` (**required**, string): The role name to be updated
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `rule-type` (optional, string, default: item-rule): item-rule, target-rule, role-rule, auth-method-rule, search-rule, reports-rule, gw-reports-rule, sra-reports-rule, ara-reports-rule, sra-rule, ara-rule
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `ttl` (optional, integer): RoleRule ttl
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Update role details
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /update-role`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/updaterole

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Role name
- `analytics-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view analytics. Currently only 'none', 'own', 'all' values are supported, allowing associated auth methods to view reports produced by the same auth methods.
- `ara-reports-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view Agentic Runtime Authority Dashboard. Currently only 'none', 'scoped', 'all' values are supported.
- `audit-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view audit logs. Currently only 'none', 'own', 'scoped' and 'all' values are supported, allowing associated auth methods to view audit logs produced by the same auth methods.
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string, default: default_comment): Description of the object
- `event-center-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view Event Center. Currently only 'none', 'scoped' and 'all' values are supported
- `event-forwarder-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to manage Event Forwarders. Currently only 'none' and 'all' values are supported.
- `gw-analytics-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view gw analytics. Currently only 'none', 'scoped', 'all' values are supported, allowing associated auth methods to view reports produced by the same auth methods.
- `isi-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to access Identity & Secrets Intelligence. Currently only 'none', 'scoped' and 'all' values are supported.
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `new-comment` (optional, string, default: default_comment): Deprecated - use description
- `new-name` (optional, string): New Role name
- `reverse-rbac-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view Reverse RBAC. Supported values: 'scoped', 'all'.
- `sra-reports-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view SRA Clusters. Currently only 'none', 'scoped', 'all' values are supported.
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `usage-reports-access` (optional, string): Allow this role to view Usage Report. Currently only 'none' and 'all' values are supported.

item.name: Targets (133)
item.description: Target/connector definitions (DB, cloud, PKI, etc.). Created on SaaS; many resolve at runtime only through a gateway.

request.description:
## Create an association between target and item
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /assoc-target-item`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/assoctargetitem

Required: name, target-name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): The item to associate
- `target-name` (**required**, string): The target to associate
- `certificate-path` (optional, string): A path on the target to store the certificate pem file (relevant only for certificate provisioning)
- `chain-path` (optional, string): A path on the target to store the full chain pem file (relevant only for certificate provisioning)
- `disable-previous-key-version` (optional, boolean, default: False): Automatically disable previous key version (required for azure targets)
- `external-key-name` (optional, string): The external key name to associate with the classic key (Relevant only for Classic Key AWS/Azure/GCP targets)
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key-operations` (optional, array): A list of allowed operations for the key (required for azure targets)
- `keyring-name` (optional, string): Keyring name of the GCP KMS (required for gcp targets)
- `kms-algorithm` (optional, string): Algorithm of the key in GCP KMS (required for gcp targets)
- `location-id` (optional, string): Location id of the GCP KMS (required for gcp targets)
- `multi-region` (optional, string, default: false): Set to 'true' to create a multi-region managed key. (Relevant only for Classic Key AWS targets)
- `post-provision-command` (optional, string): A custom command to run on the remote target after successful provisioning (relevant only for certificate provisioning)
- `private-key-path` (optional, string): A path on the target to store the private key (relevant only for certificate provisioning)
- `project-id` (optional, string): Project id of the GCP KMS (required for gcp targets)
- `protection-level` (optional, string, default: software): Protection level of the key [software/hardware] (relevant for gcp targets)
- `purpose` (optional, string): Purpose of the key in GCP KMS (required for gcp targets)
- `regions` (optional, array): The list of regions to create a copy of the key in (relevant for aws targets)
- `sra-association` (optional, boolean, default: False): Is the target to associate is for sra, relevant only for linked target association for ldap rotated secret
- `tenant-secret-type` (optional, string): The tenant secret type [Data/SearchIndex/Analytics] (required for salesforce targets)
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `vault-name` (optional, string): Name of the vault used (required for azure targets)

## Creates a new Artifactory target
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /target-create-artifactory._

`POST /create-artifactory-target`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createartifactorytarget

Required: artifactory-admin-name, artifactory-admin-pwd, base-url, name

Fields:
- `artifactory-admin-name` (**required**, string): Artifactory Admin Name
- `artifactory-admin-pwd` (**required**, string): Artifactory Admin password
- `base-url` (**required**, string): Base URL
- `name` (**required**, string): Target name
- `comment` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key` (optional, string): The name of a key used to encrypt the target secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Creates a new aws target
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /target-create-aws._

`POST /create-aws-target`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createawstarget

Required: access-key, access-key-id, name

Fields:
- `access-key` (**required**, string): AWS secret access key
- `access-key-id` (**required**, string): AWS access key ID
- `name` (**required**, string): Target name
- `comment` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `generate-external-id` (optional, boolean): A unique auto-generated value used in your AWS account when configuring your AWS IAM role to securely delegate access to Akeyless. Relevant only when using GW cloud ID
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the target secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `region` (optional, string, default: us-east-2): AWS region
- `role-arn` (optional, string): AWS IAM role identifier that Gateway will assume in your AWS account, relevant only when using external ID
- `session-token` (optional, string): Required only for temporary security credentials retrieved using STS
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-gw-cloud-identity` (optional, boolean): Use the GW's Cloud IAM

## Creates a new azure target
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /target-create-azure._

`POST /create-azure-target`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createazuretarget

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Target name
- `azure-cloud` (optional, string, default: AzureCloud): Azure cloud environment to use. Values: AzureCloud (default), AzureUSGovernment, AzureChinaCloud.
- `client-id` (optional, string): Azure client/application id
- `client-secret` (optional, string): Azure client secret
- `comment` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `connection-type` (optional, string, default: credentials): Type of connection [credentials/cloud-identity]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the target secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `resource-group-name` (optional, string): The Resource Group name in your Azure subscription
- `resource-name` (optional, string): The name of the relevant Resource
- `subscription-id` (optional, string): Azure Subscription Id
- `tenant-id` (optional, string): Azure tenant id
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-gw-cloud-identity` (optional, boolean): Use the GW's Cloud IAM [Deprecated: Use connection-type=cloud-identity]

## Creates a new db target
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /target-create-db._

`POST /create-db-target`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createdbtarget

Required: connection-type, db-type, name

Fields:
- `connection-type` (**required**, string, default: credentials): Type of connection to mssql database [credentials/cloud-identity/wallet/parent-target]
- `db-type` (**required**, string)
- `name` (**required**, string): Target name
- `azure-client-id` (optional, string): (Optional) Client id (relevant for "cloud-service-provider" only)
- `azure-client-secret` (optional, string): (Optional) Client secret (relevant for "cloud-service-provider" only)
- `azure-tenant-id` (optional, string): (Optional) Tenant id (relevant for "cloud-service-provider" only)
- `client-certificate` (optional, string): Client certificate for mutual TLS

## Updates an existing DigiCert target
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /target-update-digicert`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/targetupdatedigicert

Required: email, name

Fields:
- `email` (**required**, string): Email address for ACME account registration
- `name` (**required**, string): Target name
- `acme-challenge` (optional, string, default: dns): ACME challenge type. Options: [dns]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `digicert-url` (optional, string, default: us-production): DigiCert ACME endpoint selector. Options: [us-production/eu-production/us-demo/eu-demo]
- `dns-target-creds` (optional, string): Name of existing cloud target for DNS credentials. Required when challenge type is dns. Supported providers: AWS, Azure, GCP, Cloudflare
- `dns-zone` (optional, string): Cloudflare DNS zone identifier. Required when DNS credentials target is Cloudflare
- `eab-hmac-key` (optional, string): External Account Binding HMAC key (required for ACME account bootstrap on create)
- `eab-key-id` (optional, string): External Account Binding key identifier (required for ACME account bootstrap on create)
- `gcp-project` (optional, string): GCP Cloud DNS project ID. Optional and can be derived from service account
- `hosted-zone` (optional, string): AWS Route53 hosted zone ID. Required when DNS credentials target is AWS
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `keep-prev-version` (optional, string): Whether to keep previous version [true/false]. If not set, use default according to account settings
- `key` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the target secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `new-name` (optional, string): New target name
- `resource-group` (optional, string): Azure resource group name. Required when DNS credentials target is Azure
- `timeout` (optional, string, default: 5m): Timeout for challenge validation
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

- `secret` (**required**, string): Secret of the api credentials to the Godaddy account
- `customer_id` (optional, string): Customer ID (ShopperId) required for renewal of imported certificates
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `imap-port` (optional, string, default: 993): ImapPort of the IMAP service
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `keep-prev-version` (optional, string): Whether to keep previous version [true/false]. If not set, use default according to account settings
- `key` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the target secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `new-name` (optional, string): New target name
- `timeout` (optional, string, default: 5m): Timeout waiting for certificate validation in Duration format (1h - 1 Hour, 20m - 20 Minutes, 33m3s - 33 Minutes and 3 Seconds), maximum 1h.
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

- `use-lower-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one lowercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (a to z). [true/false]
- `use-numbers` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one numeric character (0 to 9). [true/false]

## Creates Ping Federate dynamic secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /dynamic-secret-create-ping`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/dynamicsecretcreateping

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Dynamic secret name
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `ping-administrative-port` (optional, string, default: 9999): Ping Federate administrative port
- `ping-atm-id` (optional, string): Set a specific Access Token Management (ATM) instance for the created OAuth Client by providing the ATM Id. If no explicit value is given, the default pingfederate server ATM will be set.
- `ping-authorization-port` (optional, string, default: 9031): Ping Federate authorization port
- `ping-cert-subject-dn` (optional, string): The subject DN of the client certificate. If no explicit value is given, the producer will create CA certificate and matched client certificate and return it as value. Used in conjunction with ping-issuer-dn (relevant for CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE authentication method)
- `ping-client-authentication-type` (optional, string, default: CLIENT_SECRET): OAuth Client Authentication Type [CLIENT_SECRET, PRIVATE_KEY_JWT, CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE]
- `ping-enforce-replay-prevention` (optional, string, default: false): Determines whether PingFederate requires a unique signed JWT from the client for each action (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method) [true/false]
- `ping-grant-types` (optional, array): List of OAuth client grant types [IMPLICIT, AUTHORIZATION_CODE, CLIENT_CREDENTIALS, TOKEN_EXCHANGE, REFRESH_TOKEN, ASSERTION_GRANTS, PASSWORD, RESOURCE_OWNER_CREDENTIALS]. If no explicit value is given, AUTHORIZATION_CODE will be selected as default.
- `ping-issuer-dn` (optional, string): Issuer DN of trusted CA certificate that imported into Ping Federate server. You may select \"Trust Any\" to trust all the existing issuers in Ping Federate server. Used in conjunction with ping-cert-subject-dn (relevant for CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE authentication method)
- `ping-jwks` (optional, string): Base64-encoded JSON Web Key Set (JWKS). If no explicit value is given, the producer will create JWKs and matched signed JWT (Sign Algo: RS256) and return it as value (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-jwks-url` (optional, string): The URL of the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS). If no explicit value is given, the producer will create JWKs and matched signed JWT and return it as value (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-password` (optional, string): Ping Federate privileged user password
- `ping-privileged-user` (optional, string): Ping Federate privileged user
- `ping-redirect-uris` (optional, array): List of URIs to which the OAuth authorization server may redirect the resource owner's user agent after authorization is obtained. At least one redirection URI is required for the AUTHORIZATION_CODE and IMPLICIT grant types.
- `ping-restricted-scopes` (optional, array): Limit the OAuth client to specific scopes list
- `ping-signing-algo` (optional, string): The signing algorithm that the client must use to sign its request objects [RS256,RS384,RS512,ES256,ES384,ES512,PS256,PS384,PS512] If no explicit value is given, the client can use any of the supported signing algorithms (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-url` (optional, string): Ping URL
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object

- `allow-subdomains` (optional, boolean): Allow subdomains
- `allowed-domains` (optional, array): Allowed domains
- `auto-generated-folder` (optional, string): Auto generated folder
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `enable-admin-rotation` (optional, boolean, default: False): Automatic admin credentials rotation
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key

- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `user-ttl` (optional, string, default: 2160h): User TTL in time.Duration format (2160h / 129600m / etc...). When using sign-using-akeyless-pki certificates created will have this validity period, otherwise the user-ttl is taken from the Validity Period field of the Zone's' Issuing Template. When using cert-manager it is advised to have a TTL of above 60 days (1440h). For more information - https://cert-manager.io/docs/usage/certificate/
- `venafi-access-token` (optional, string): Venafi Access Token to use to access the TPP environment (Relevant when using TPP)
- `venafi-api-key` (optional, string): Venafi API key
- `venafi-baseurl` (optional, string): Venafi Baseurl
- `venafi-client-id` (optional, string, default: akeyless): Venafi Client ID that was used when the access token was generated
- `venafi-refresh-token` (optional, string): Venafi Refresh Token to use when the Access Token is expired (Relevant when using TPP)
- `venafi-use-tpp` (optional, boolean): Venafi using TPP
- `venafi-zone` (optional, string): Venafi Zone

## Updates Ping Federate dynamic secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /dynamic-secret-update-ping`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/dynamicsecretupdateping

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Dynamic secret name
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `new-name` (optional, string): Dynamic secret name
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `ping-administrative-port` (optional, string, default: 9999): Ping Federate administrative port
- `ping-atm-id` (optional, string): Set a specific Access Token Management (ATM) instance for the created OAuth Client by providing the ATM Id. If no explicit value is given, the default pingfederate server ATM will be set.
- `ping-authorization-port` (optional, string, default: 9031): Ping Federate authorization port
- `ping-cert-subject-dn` (optional, string): The subject DN of the client certificate. If no explicit value is given, the producer will create CA certificate and matched client certificate and return it as value. Used in conjunction with ping-issuer-dn (relevant for CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE authentication method)
- `ping-client-authentication-type` (optional, string, default: CLIENT_SECRET): OAuth Client Authentication Type [CLIENT_SECRET, PRIVATE_KEY_JWT, CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE]
- `ping-enforce-replay-prevention` (optional, string, default: false): Determines whether PingFederate requires a unique signed JWT from the client for each action (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method) [true/false]
- `ping-grant-types` (optional, array): List of OAuth client grant types [IMPLICIT, AUTHORIZATION_CODE, CLIENT_CREDENTIALS, TOKEN_EXCHANGE, REFRESH_TOKEN, ASSERTION_GRANTS, PASSWORD, RESOURCE_OWNER_CREDENTIALS]. If no explicit value is given, AUTHORIZATION_CODE will be selected as default.
- `ping-issuer-dn` (optional, string): Issuer DN of trusted CA certificate that imported into Ping Federate server. You may select \"Trust Any\" to trust all the existing issuers in Ping Federate server. Used in conjunction with ping-cert-subject-dn (relevant for CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE authentication method)
- `ping-jwks` (optional, string): Base64-encoded JSON Web Key Set (JWKS). If no explicit value is given, the producer will create JWKs and matched signed JWT (Sign Algo: RS256) and return it as value (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-jwks-url` (optional, string): The URL of the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS). If no explicit value is given, the producer will create JWKs and matched signed JWT and return it as value (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-password` (optional, string): Ping Federate privileged user password
- `ping-privileged-user` (optional, string): Ping Federate privileged user
- `ping-redirect-uris` (optional, array): List of URIs to which the OAuth authorization server may redirect the resource owner's user agent after authorization is obtained. At least one redirection URI is required for the AUTHORIZATION_CODE and IMPLICIT grant types.
- `ping-restricted-scopes` (optional, array): Limit the OAuth client to specific scopes list
- `ping-signing-algo` (optional, string): The signing algorithm that the client must use to sign its request objects [RS256,RS384,RS512,ES256,ES384,ES512,PS256,PS384,PS512] If no explicit value is given, the client can use any of the supported signing algorithms (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-url` (optional, string): Ping URL
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target-name` (optional, string): Target name
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `user-ttl` (optional, string, default: 60m): The time from dynamic secret creation to expiration.

- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `new-name` (optional, string): Dynamic secret name
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key
- `root-first-in-chain` (optional, boolean): Root first in chain
- `sign-using-akeyless-pki` (optional, boolean): Use Akeyless PKI issuer or Venafi issuer
- `signer-key-name` (optional, string): Signer key name
- `store-private-key` (optional, boolean): Store private key
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target-name` (optional, string): Target name
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `user-ttl` (optional, string, default: 2160h): User TTL in time.Duration format (2160h / 129600m / etc...). When using sign-using-akeyless-pki certificates created will have this validity period, otherwise the user-ttl is taken from the Validity Period field of the Zone's' Issuing Template. When using cert-manager it is advised to have a TTL of above 60 days (1440h). For more information - https://cert-manager.io/docs/usage/certificate/
- `venafi-access-token` (optional, string): Venafi Access Token to use to access the TPP environment (Relevant when using TPP)
- `venafi-api-key` (optional, string): Venafi API key
- `venafi-baseurl` (optional, string): Venafi Baseurl
- `venafi-client-id` (optional, string, default: akeyless): Venafi Client ID that was used when the access token was generated
- `venafi-refresh-token` (optional, string): Venafi Refresh Token to use when the Access Token is expired (Relevant when using TPP)
- `venafi-use-tpp` (optional, boolean): Venafi using TPP
- `venafi-zone` (optional, string): Venafi Zone

item.name: Rotated Secrets (50)
item.description: Rotated secrets. Created on SaaS; rotation and value fetch run through a gateway.

request.description:
`POST /create-rotated-secret`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createrotatedsecret

Required: name, rotator-type, target-name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Secret name
- `rotator-type` (**required**, string): Rotator Type
- `target-name` (**required**, string): Target name
- `ProviderType` (optional, string)
- `api-id` (optional, string): API ID to rotate (relevant only for rotator-type=api-key)
- `api-key` (optional, string): API key to rotate (relevant only for rotator-type=api-key)
- `application-id` (optional, string): ApplicationId (used in azure)
- `authentication-credentials` (optional, string, default: use-user-creds): The credentials to connect with use-user-creds/use-target-creds
- `auto-rotate` (optional, string): Whether to automatically rotate every --rotation-interval days, or disable existing automatic rotation [true/false]
- `aws-region` (optional, string, default: us-east-2): Aws Region (relevant only for aws)
- `custom-payload` (optional, string): Secret payload to be sent with rotation request (relevant only for rotator-type=custom)
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `gcp-key` (optional, string): Base64-encoded service account private key text
- `gcp-service-account-email` (optional, string): The email of the gcp service account to rotate
- `gcp-service-account-key-id` (optional, string): The key id of the gcp service account to rotate
- `grace-rotation` (optional, string): Create a new access key without deleting the old key from AWS for backup (relevant only for AWS) [true/false]
- `host-provider` (optional, string): Host provider type [explicit/target], Default Host provider is explicit, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access of ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `lock-during-sra-session` (optional, string): Lock this secret for read/update while an SRA session is active
- `metadata` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `password-length` (optional, string): The length of the password to be generated
- `rotate-after-disconnect` (optional, string): StringOrBool accepts JSON strings, booleans, and numbers for backward compatibility with older SDK versions that send boolean values for rotate-after-disconnect.
- `rotated-password` (optional, string): rotated-username password (relevant only for rotator-type=password)
- `rotated-username` (optional, string): username to be rotated, if selected use-self-creds at rotator-creds-type, this username will try to rotate it's own password, if use-target-creds is selected, target credentials will be use to rotate the rotated-password (relevant only for rotator-type=password)
- `rotation-hour` (optional, integer): The Hour of the rotation in UTC. Default rotation-hour is 14:00
- `rotation-interval` (optional, string): The number of days to wait between every automatic key rotation (1-365)
- `rotator-creds-type` (optional, string)
- `rotator-custom-cmd` (optional, string): Custom rotation command (relevant only for ssh target)
- `same-password` (optional, string): Rotate same password for each host from the Linked Target (relevant only for Linked Target)
- `secure-access-allow-external-user` (optional, boolean, default: False): Allow providing external user for a domain users (relevant only for rdp)
- `secure-access-aws-account-id` (optional, string): The AWS account id (relevant only for aws)
- `secure-access-aws-native-cli` (optional, boolean): The AWS native cli
- `secure-access-bastion-issuer` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-certificate-issuer
- `secure-access-certificate-issuer` (optional, string): Path to the SSH Certificate Issuer for your Akeyless Secure Access
- `secure-access-db-name` (optional, string): The DB name (relevant only for DB Dynamic-Secret)
- `secure-access-db-schema` (optional, string): The db schema (relevant only for mssql or postgresql)
- `secure-access-disable-concurrent-connections` (optional, boolean): Enable this flag to prevent simultaneous use of the same secret
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `secure-access-host` (optional, array): Target servers for connections (In case of Linked Target association, host(s) will inherit Linked Target hosts - Relevant only for Dynamic Secrets/producers)
- `secure-access-rdp-domain` (optional, string): Required when the Dynamic Secret is used for a domain user (relevant only for RDP Dynamic-Secret)
- `secure-access-rdp-user` (optional, string): Override the RDP Domain username (relevant only for rdp)
- `secure-access-url` (optional, string): Destination URL to inject secrets
- `secure-access-web` (optional, boolean, default: False): Enable Web Secure Remote Access
- `secure-access-web-browsing` (optional, boolean, default: False): Secure browser viaAkeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA) (relevant only for aws or azure)
- `secure-access-web-proxy` (optional, boolean, default: False): Web-Proxy via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA) (relevant only for aws or azure)
- `ssh-password` (optional, string): Deprecated: use RotatedPassword
- `ssh-username` (optional, string): Deprecated: use RotatedUser
- `storage-account-key-name` (optional, string): The name of the storage account key to rotate [key1/key2/kerb1/kerb2] (relevat to azure-storage-account)
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target` (optional, array): A list of linked targets to be associated, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access for ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret, To specify multiple targets use argument multiple times
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `user-attribute` (optional, string, default: cn): LDAP User Attribute, Default value "cn"
- `user-dn` (optional, string): LDAP User Base DN

- `lock-during-sra-session` (optional, string): Lock this secret for read/update while an SRA session is active
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `password-length` (optional, string): The length of the password to be generated

- `rotation-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the rotation of the item would you like to be notified
- `rotation-hour` (optional, integer)
- `rotation-interval` (optional, string)
- `secure-access-aws-account-id` (optional, string): The AWS account id
- `secure-access-aws-native-cli` (optional, boolean): The AWS native cli
- `secure-access-bastion-issuer` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-certificate-issuer
- `secure-access-certificate-issuer` (optional, string): Path to the SSH Certificate Issuer for your Akeyless Secure Access
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)

- `use-lower-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one lowercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (a to z). [true/false]

`POST /rotated-secret-create-gcp`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/rotatedsecretcreategcp

Required: name, rotator-type, target-name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Rotated secret name
- `rotator-type` (**required**, string): The rotator type. options: [target/service-account-rotator]
- `target-name` (**required**, string): The target name to associate
- `authentication-credentials` (optional, string, default: use-user-creds): The credentials to connect with use-user-creds/use-target-creds
- `auto-rotate` (optional, string)
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `gcp-key` (optional, string): Base64-encoded service account private key text
- `gcp-service-account-email` (optional, string): The email of the gcp service account to rotate
- `gcp-service-account-key-id` (optional, string): The key id of the gcp service account to rotate
- `grace-rotation` (optional, string): Enable graceful rotation (keep both versions temporarily). When enabled, a new secret version is created while the previous version is kept for the grace period, so both versions exist for a limited time. [true/false]
- `grace-rotation-hour` (optional, integer): The Hour of the grace rotation in UTC
- `grace-rotation-interval` (optional, string): The number of days to wait before deleting the old key (must be bigger than rotation-interval)

- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-capital-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one uppercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (A to Z). [true/false]

## Creates new ldap rotated secret item.
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /rotated-secret-create-ldap`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/rotatedsecretcreateldap

Required: name, rotator-type, target-name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Rotated secret name
- `rotator-type` (**required**, string): The rotator type. options: [target/ldap]
- `target-name` (**required**, string): The target name to associate
- `ProviderType` (optional, string)
- `authentication-credentials` (optional, string, default: use-user-creds): The credentials to connect with use-user-creds/use-target-creds
- `auto-rotate` (optional, string)
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `host-provider` (optional, string): Host provider type [explicit/target], Default Host provider is explicit, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access of ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input)
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key` (optional, string)
- `lock-during-sra-session` (optional, string): Lock this secret for read/update while an SRA session is active
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `password-length` (optional, string): The length of the password to be generated
- `rotate-after-disconnect` (optional, string): StringOrBool accepts JSON strings, booleans, and numbers for backward compatibility with older SDK versions that send boolean values for rotate-after-disconnect.
- `rotated-password` (optional, string): rotated-username password (relevant only for rotator-type=ldap)
- `rotated-username` (optional, string): username to be rotated, if selected use-self-creds at rotator-creds-type, this username will try to rotate it's own password, if use-target-creds is selected, target credentials will be use to rotate the rotated-password (relevant only for rotator-type=ldap)
- `rotation-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the rotation of the item would you like to be notified
- `rotation-hour` (optional, integer)
- `rotation-interval` (optional, string)
- `secure-access-bastion-issuer` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-certificate-issuer
- `secure-access-certificate-issuer` (optional, string): Path to the SSH Certificate Issuer for your Akeyless Secure Access
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `secure-access-host` (optional, array): Target servers for connections (In case of Linked Target association, host(s) will inherit Linked Target hosts - Relevant only for Dynamic Secrets/producers)
- `secure-access-rdp-domain` (optional, string): Default domain name server. i.e. microsoft.com
- `secure-access-url` (optional, string): Destination URL to inject secrets
- `secure-access-web` (optional, boolean, default: False): Enable Web Secure Remote Access
- `secure-access-web-browsing` (optional, boolean, default: False): Secure browser via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `secure-access-web-proxy` (optional, boolean, default: False): Web-Proxy via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target` (optional, array): A list of linked targets to be associated, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access for ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret, To specify multiple targets use argument multiple times
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-capital-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one uppercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (A to Z). [true/false]
- `use-lower-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one lowercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (a to z). [true/false]
- `use-numbers` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one numeric character (0 to 9). [true/false]
- `use-special-characters` (optional, string)
- `user-attribute` (optional, string, default: cn): LDAP User Attribute, Default value "cn"
- `user-dn` (optional, string): Base DN to Perform User Search

## Updates new aws rotated secret item.
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /rotated-secret-update-aws`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/rotatedsecretupdateaws

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Rotated secret name
- `add-tag` (optional, array): List of the new tags that will be attached to this item
- `api-id` (optional, string): API ID to rotate (relevant only for rotator-type=api-key)
- `api-key` (optional, string): API key to rotate (relevant only for rotator-type=api-key)
- `authentication-credentials` (optional, string, default: use-user-creds): The credentials to connect with use-user-creds/use-target-creds
- `auto-rotate` (optional, string)
- `aws-region` (optional, string, default: us-east-2): Aws Region
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string, default: default_metadata): Description of the object
- `grace-rotation` (optional, string): Enable graceful rotation (keep both versions temporarily). When enabled, a new secret version is created while the previous version is kept for the grace period, so both versions exist for a limited time. [true/false]
- `grace-rotation-hour` (optional, integer): The Hour of the grace rotation in UTC
- `grace-rotation-interval` (optional, string): The number of days to wait before deleting the old key (must be bigger than rotation-interval)
- `grace-rotation-timing` (optional, string): When to create the new version relative to the rotation date [after/before]
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input)
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `keep-prev-version` (optional, string): Whether to keep previous version [true/false]. If not set, use default according to account settings
- `key` (optional, string)
- `lock-during-sra-session` (optional, string): Lock this secret for read/update while an SRA session is active
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `new-name` (optional, string): New item name
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `password-length` (optional, string): The length of the password to be generated
- `rm-tag` (optional, array): List of the existent tags that will be removed from this item
- `rotate-after-disconnect` (optional, string): StringOrBool accepts JSON strings, booleans, and numbers for backward compatibility with older SDK versions that send boolean values for rotate-after-disconnect.
- `rotation-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the rotation of the item would you like to be notified
- `rotation-hour` (optional, integer)
- `rotation-interval` (optional, string)
- `secure-access-aws-account-id` (optional, string): The AWS account id
- `secure-access-aws-native-cli` (optional, boolean): The AWS native cli
- `secure-access-bastion-issuer` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-certificate-issuer
- `secure-access-certificate-issuer` (optional, string): Path to the SSH Certificate Issuer for your Akeyless Secure Access
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-capital-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one uppercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (A to Z). [true/false]
- `use-lower-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one lowercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (a to z). [true/false]
- `use-numbers` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one numeric character (0 to 9). [true/false]
- `use-special-characters` (optional, string)
