## Updates new ldap rotated secret item.
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /rotated-secret-update-ldap`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/rotatedsecretupdateldap

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Rotated secret name
- `ProviderType` (optional, string)
- `add-tag` (optional, array): List of the new tags that will be attached to this item
- `authentication-credentials` (optional, string, default: use-user-creds): The credentials to connect with use-user-creds/use-target-creds
- `auto-rotate` (optional, string)
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string, default: default_metadata): Description of the object
- `host-provider` (optional, string): Host provider type [explicit/target], Default Host provider is explicit, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access of ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input)
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `keep-prev-version` (optional, string): Whether to keep previous version [true/false]. If not set, use default according to account settings
- `key` (optional, string)
- `lock-during-sra-session` (optional, string): Lock this secret for read/update while an SRA session is active
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Set the maximum number of versions, limited by the account settings defaults.
- `new-name` (optional, string): New item name
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `password-length` (optional, string): The length of the password to be generated
- `rm-tag` (optional, array): List of the existent tags that will be removed from this item
- `rotate-after-disconnect` (optional, string): StringOrBool accepts JSON strings, booleans, and numbers for backward compatibility with older SDK versions that send boolean values for rotate-after-disconnect.
- `rotated-password` (optional, string): rotated-username password (relevant only for rotator-type=ldap)
- `rotated-username` (optional, string): username to be rotated, if selected use-self-creds at rotator-creds-type, this username will try to rotate it's own password, if use-target-creds is selected, target credentials will be use to rotate the rotated-password (relevant only for rotator-type=ldap)
- `rotation-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the rotation of the item would you like to be notified
- `rotation-hour` (optional, integer)
- `rotation-interval` (optional, string)
- `secure-access-bastion-issuer` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-certificate-issuer
- `secure-access-certificate-issuer` (optional, string): Path to the SSH Certificate Issuer for your Akeyless Secure Access
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `secure-access-host` (optional, array): Target servers for connections (In case of Linked Target association, host(s) will inherit Linked Target hosts - Relevant only for Dynamic Secrets/producers)
- `secure-access-rdp-domain` (optional, string): Default domain name server. i.e. microsoft.com
- `secure-access-url` (optional, string): Destination URL to inject secrets
- `secure-access-web` (optional, boolean, default: False): Enable Web Secure Remote Access
- `secure-access-web-browsing` (optional, boolean, default: False): Secure browser via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `secure-access-web-proxy` (optional, boolean, default: False): Web-Proxy via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `target` (optional, array): A list of linked targets to be associated, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access for ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret, To specify multiple targets use argument multiple times
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-capital-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one uppercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (A to Z). [true/false]
- `use-lower-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one lowercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (a to z). [true/false]
- `use-numbers` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one numeric character (0 to 9). [true/false]
- `use-special-characters` (optional, string)
- `user-attribute` (optional, string, default: cn): LDAP User Attribute, Default value "cn"
- `user-dn` (optional, string): Base DN to Perform User Search

- `new-version` (optional, boolean): Deprecated
- `rm-tag` (optional, array): List of the existent tags that will be removed from this item
- `rotate-after-disconnect` (optional, string): StringOrBool accepts JSON strings, booleans, and numbers for backward compatibility with older SDK versions that send boolean values for rotate-after-disconnect.
- `rotated-password` (optional, string): rotated-username password

- `rotation-hour` (optional, integer): The Hour of the rotation in UTC
- `rotation-interval` (optional, string): The number of days to wait between every automatic key rotation (7-365)
- `rotator-creds-type` (optional, string, default: use-self-creds): The credentials to connect with use-self-creds/use-target-creds
- `rotator-custom-cmd` (optional, string): "Custom rotation command (relevant only for ssh target)

- `secure-access-aws-native-cli` (optional, boolean): The AWS native cli

- `secure-access-certificate-issuer` (optional, string): Path to the SSH Certificate Issuer for your Akeyless Secure Access

- `ssh-password` (optional, string): Deprecated: use RotatedPassword
- `ssh-username` (optional, string): Deprecated: use RotatedUser
- `storage-account-key-name` (optional, string): The name of the storage account key to rotate [key1/key2/kerb1/kerb2]
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `user-attribute` (optional, string, default: cn): LDAP User Attribute, Default value "cn"
- `user-dn` (optional, string): LDAP User Base DN

item.name: Gateway (117)
item.description: Gateway configuration and runtime. These require a running gateway and error against public SaaS.

request.description:
## Creates Allowed Access
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /gateway-create-allowed-access`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewaycreateallowedaccess

Required: access-id, name

Fields:
- `access-id` (**required**, string): Access ID The access id to be attached to this allowed access. Auth method with this access id should already exist.
- `name` (**required**, string): Allowed access name
- `SubClaimsCaseInsensitive` (optional, boolean)
- `case-sensitive` (optional, string, default: true): Treat sub claims as case-sensitive [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Allowed access description
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `permissions` (optional, string): Permissions  Comma-seperated list of permissions for this allowed access. Available permissions: [defaults,targets,classic_keys,automatic_migration,ldap_auth,dynamic_secret,k8s_auth,log_forwarding,zero_knowledge_encryption,rotated_secret,caching,event_forwarding,admin,kmip,general,rotate_secret_value]
- `sub-claims` (optional, object): Sub claims key/val of sub claims, e.g group=admins,developers
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Creates K8S Auth config
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /gateway-create-k8s-auth-config`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewaycreatek8sauthconfig

Required: access-id, k8s-host, name, signing-key

Fields:
- `access-id` (**required**, string): The access ID of the Kubernetes auth method
- `k8s-host` (**required**, string): The URL of the kubernetes API server
- `name` (**required**, string): K8S Auth config name
- `signing-key` (**required**, string): The private key (base64 encoded) associated with the public key defined in the Kubernetes auth
- `cluster-api-type` (optional, string, default: native_k8s): Cluster access type. options: [native_k8s, rancher]
- `disable-issuer-validation` (optional, string): Disable issuer validation [true/false]
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `k8s-auth-type` (optional, string, default: token): K8S auth type [token/certificate]. (relevant for "native_k8s" only)
- `k8s-ca-cert` (optional, string): The CA Certificate (base64 encoded) to use to call into the kubernetes API server
- `k8s-client-certificate` (optional, string): Content of the k8 client certificate (PEM format) in a Base64 format (relevant for "native_k8s" only)
- `k8s-client-key` (optional, string): Content of the k8 client private key (PEM format) in a Base64 format (relevant for "native_k8s" only)
- `k8s-issuer` (optional, string, default: kubernetes/serviceaccount): The Kubernetes JWT issuer name. K8SIssuer is the claim that specifies who issued the Kubernetes token
- `rancher-api-key` (optional, string): The api key used to access the TokenReview API to validate other JWTs (relevant for "rancher" only)
- `rancher-cluster-id` (optional, string): The cluster id as define in rancher (relevant for "rancher" only)
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `token-exp` (optional, integer, default: 300): Time in seconds of expiration of the Akeyless Kube Auth Method token
- `token-reviewer-jwt` (optional, string): A Kubernetes service account JWT used to access the TokenReview API to validate other JWTs (relevant for "native_k8s" only). If not set, the JWT submitted in the authentication process will be used to access the Kubernetes TokenReview API.
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-gw-service-account` (optional, boolean): Use the GW's service account

## Create migration
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /gateway-create-migration`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewaycreatemigration

Required: hosts, name, si-target-name, si-users-path-template, target-location

Fields:
- `hosts` (**required**, string): A comma separated list of IPs, CIDR ranges, or DNS names to scan
- `name` (**required**, string): Migration name
- `si-target-name` (**required**, string): SSH, Windows or Linked Target Name. (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-users-path-template` (**required**, string): Path location template for migrating users as Rotated Secrets e.g.: .../Users/{{COMPUTER_NAME}}/{{USERNAME}} (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `target-location` (**required**, string): Target location in Akeyless for imported secrets
- `ServiceAccountKeyDecoded` (optional, string)
- `ad-auto-rotate` (optional, string): Enable/Disable automatic/recurrent rotation for migrated secrets. Default is false: only manual rotation is allowed for migrated secrets. If set to true, this command should be combined with --ad-rotation-interval and --ad-rotation-hour parameters (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-cert-expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of discovered certificates would you like to be notified (Relevant only for Active Directory migration with certificate discovery enabled)
- `ad-certificates-path-template` (optional, string): Path location template for migrating certificates e.g.: /Certificates/{{COMMON_NAME}} (Relevant only for Active Directory migration with certificate discovery enabled)
- `ad-computer-base-dn` (optional, string): Distinguished Name of Computer objects (servers) to search in Active Directory e.g.: CN=Computers,DC=example,DC=com (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-discover-iis-app` (optional, string, default: false): Enable/Disable discovery of IIS application from each domain server as part of the SSH/Windows Rotated Secrets. Default is false. (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-discover-services` (optional, string, default: false): Enable/Disable discovery of Windows services from each domain server as part of the SSH/Windows Rotated Secrets. Default is false. (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-discovery-types` (optional, array): Set migration discovery types (domain-users, computers, local-users). (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-domain-name` (optional, string): Active Directory Domain Name (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-domain-users-path-template` (optional, string): Path location template for migrating domain users as Rotated Secrets e.g.: .../DomainUsers/{{USERNAME}} (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-local-users-ignore` (optional, string): Comma-separated list of Local Users which should not be migrated (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-local-users-path-template` (optional, string): Path location template for migrating domain users as Rotated Secrets e.g.: .../LocalUsers/{{COMPUTER_NAME}}/{{USERNAME}} (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-os-filter` (optional, string): Filter by Operating System to run the migration, can be used with wildcards, e.g. SRV20* (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-rotation-hour` (optional, integer): The hour of the scheduled rotation in UTC (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-rotation-interval` (optional, integer): The number of days to wait between every automatic rotation [1-365] (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-sra-enable-rdp` (optional, string): Enable/Disable RDP Secure Remote Access for the migrated local users rotated secrets. Default is false: rotated secrets will not be created with SRA (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-ssh-port` (optional, string, default: 22): Set the SSH Port for further connection to the domain servers. Default is port 22 (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-target-format` (optional, string, default: linked): Relevant only for ad-discovery-types=computers. For linked, all computers will be migrated into a linked target(s). if set with regular, the migration will create a target for each computer.
- `ad-target-name` (optional, string): Active Directory LDAP Target Name. Server type should be Active Directory (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-targets-path-template` (optional, string): Path location template for migrating domain servers as SSH/Windows Targets e.g.: .../Servers/{{COMPUTER_NAME}} (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-targets-type` (optional, string, default: windows): Set the target type of the domain servers [ssh/windows](Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-user-base-dn` (optional, string): Distinguished Name of User objects to search in Active Directory, e.g.: CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-user-groups` (optional, string): Comma-separated list of domain groups from which privileged domain users will be migrated. If empty, migrate all users based on the --ad-user-base-dn (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-winrm-over-http` (optional, string, default: false): Use WinRM over HTTP, by default runs over HTTPS
- `ad-winrm-port` (optional, string, default: 5986): Set the WinRM Port for further connection to the domain servers. Default is 5986 (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad_discover_local_users` (optional, string): Enable/Disable discovery of local users from each domain server and migrate them as SSH/Windows Rotated Secrets. Default is false: only domain users will be migrated. Discovery of local users might require further installation of SSH on the servers, based on the supplied computer base DN. This will be implemented automatically as part of the migration process (Relevant only for Active Directory migration) Deprecated: use AdDiscoverTypes
- `ai-certificate-discovery` (optional, string): Enable AI-assisted certificate discovery (only when AI Insight is enabled on the Gateway)
- `aws-key` (optional, string): AWS Secret Access Key (relevant only for AWS migration)
- `aws-key-id` (optional, string): AWS Access Key ID with sufficient permissions to get all secrets, e.g. 'arn:aws:secretsmanager:[Region]:[AccountId]:secret:[/path/to/secrets/*]' (relevant only for AWS migration)
- `aws-region` (optional, string, default: us-east-2): AWS region of the required Secrets Manager (relevant only for AWS migration)
- `azure-client-id` (optional, string): Azure Key Vault Access client ID, should be Azure AD App with a service principal (relevant only for Azure Key Vault migration)
- `azure-kv-name` (optional, string): Azure Key Vault Name (relevant only for Azure Key Vault migration)
- `azure-secret` (optional, string): Azure Key Vault secret (relevant only for Azure Key Vault migration)
- `azure-tenant-id` (optional, string): Azure Key Vault Access tenant ID (relevant only for Azure Key Vault migration)
- `conjur-account` (optional, string): Conjur account name set on your Conjur server (relevant only for Conjur migration).
- `conjur-api-key` (optional, string): Conjur API Key for the specified user (relevant only for Conjur migration).
- `conjur-url` (optional, string): Conjur server base URL (relevant only for Conjur migration). If conjur-url is HTTPS and Conjur uses a private CA/self-signed certificate, make the CA bundle available on the Gateway and set CONJUR_SSL_CERT_PATH to its path.
- `conjur-username` (optional, string): Conjur username used to authenticate (relevant only for Conjur migration).
- `delete-remote` (optional, boolean): Delete the secret from the remote target as well, relevant only when usc-name is not empty (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration)
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the certificate would you like to be notified.
- `gcp-key` (optional, string): Base64-encoded GCP Service Account private key text with sufficient permissions to Secrets Manager, Minimum required permission is Secret Manager Secret Accessor, e.g. 'roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor' (relevant only for GCP migration)
- `gcp-project-id` (optional, string): GCP Project ID (cross-project override)
- `hashi-json` (optional, string, default: true): Import secret key as json value or independent secrets (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration) [true/false]
- `hashi-metadata-mode` (optional, string): Controls the amount of HashiCorp Vault secret metadata migrated with each secret value. Options: none|minimal|full
- `hashi-ns` (optional, array): HashiCorp Vault Namespaces is a comma-separated list of namespaces which need to be imported into Akeyless Vault. For every provided namespace, all its child namespaces are imported as well, e.g. nmsp/subnmsp1/subnmsp2,nmsp/anothernmsp. By default, import all namespaces (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration)
- `hashi-token` (optional, string): HashiCorp Vault access token with sufficient permissions to preform list & read operations on secrets objects (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration)
- `hashi-url` (optional, string): HashiCorp Vault API URL, e.g. https://vault-mgr01:8200 (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration)
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `k8s-ca-certificate` (optional, array): For Certificate Authentication method K8s Cluster CA certificate (relevant only for K8s migration with Certificate Authentication method)
- `k8s-client-certificate` (optional, array): K8s Client certificate with sufficient permission to list and get secrets in the namespace(s) you selected (relevant only for K8s migration with Certificate Authentication method)
- `k8s-client-key` (optional, array): K8s Client key (relevant only for K8s migration with Certificate Authentication method)
- `k8s-namespace` (optional, string): K8s Namespace, Use this field to import secrets from a particular namespace only. By default, the secrets are imported from all namespaces (relevant only for K8s migration)
- `k8s-password` (optional, string): K8s Client password (relevant only for K8s migration with Password Authentication method)
- `k8s-skip-system` (optional, boolean): K8s Skip Control Plane Secrets, This option allows to avoid importing secrets from system namespaces (relevant only for K8s migration)
- `k8s-token` (optional, string): For Token Authentication method K8s Bearer Token with sufficient permission to list and get secrets in the namespace(s) you selected (relevant only for K8s migration with Token Authentication method)
- `k8s-url` (optional, string): K8s API Server URL, e.g. https://k8s-api.mycompany.com:6443 (relevant only for K8s migration)
- `k8s-username` (optional, string): For Password Authentication method K8s Client username with sufficient permission to list and get secrets in the namespace(s) you selected (relevant only for K8s migration with Password Authentication method)
- `port-ranges` (optional, string, default: 443): A comma separated list of port ranges Examples: "80,443" or "80,443,8080-8090" or "443"
- `protection-key` (optional, string): The name of the key that protects the classic key value (if empty, the account default key will be used)
- `si-auto-rotate` (optional, string): Enable/Disable automatic/recurrent rotation for migrated secrets. Default is false: only manual rotation is allowed for migrated secrets. If set to true, this command should be combined with --si-rotation-interval and --si-rotation-hour parameters (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-rotation-hour` (optional, integer): The hour of the scheduled rotation in UTC (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-rotation-interval` (optional, integer): The number of days to wait between every automatic rotation [1-365] (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-sra-enable-rdp` (optional, string, default: false): Enable/Disable RDP Secure Remote Access for the migrated local users rotated secrets. Default is false: rotated secrets will not be created with SRA (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-user-groups` (optional, string): Comma-separated list of groups to migrate users from. If empty, all users from all groups will be migrated (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-users-ignore` (optional, string): Comma-separated list of Local Users which should not be migrated (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `type` (optional, string): Migration type (hashi/aws/gcp/k8s/azure_kv/conjur/active_directory/server_inventory/certificate)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `usc-name` (optional, string): Universal Secret Connector name
- `use-gw-cloud-identity` (optional, boolean): Use the GW's Cloud IAM

## Creates Redis dynamic secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /dynamic-secret-create-redis._

`POST /gateway-create-producer-Redis`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewaycreateproducerredis

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Dynamic secret name
- `acl-rules` (optional, string): A JSON array list of redis ACL rules to attach to the created user. For available rules see the ACL CAT command https://redis.io/commands/acl-cat By default the user will have permissions to read all keys '["~*", "+@read"]'
- `custom-username-template` (optional, string): Customize how temporary usernames are generated using go template
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `host` (optional, string, default: 127.0.0.1): Redis Host
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `password` (optional, string): Redis Password
- `password-length` (optional, string): The length of the password to be generated
- `port` (optional, string, default: 6379): Redis Port
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key
- `ssl` (optional, boolean, default: False): Enable/Disable SSL [true/false]
- `ssl-certificate` (optional, string): SSL CA certificate in base64 encoding generated from a trusted Certificate Authority (CA)
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target-name` (optional, string): Target name
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-capital-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one uppercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (A to Z). [true/false]
- `use-lower-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one lowercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (a to z). [true/false]
- `use-numbers` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one numeric character (0 to 9). [true/false]
- `use-special-characters` (optional, string)
- `user-ttl` (optional, string, default: 60m): User TTL
- `username` (optional, string): Redis Username

## Creates Artifactory dynamic secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /dynamic-secret-create-artifactory._

`POST /gateway-create-producer-artifactory`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewaycreateproducerartifactory

Required: artifactory-token-audience, artifactory-token-scope, name

Fields:

- `artifactory-token-scope` (**required**, string): Token Scope

- `artifactory-admin-name` (optional, string): Artifactory Admin Name
- `artifactory-admin-pwd` (optional, string): Artifactory Admin password
- `base-url` (optional, string): Base URL
- `custom-username-template` (optional, string): Customize how temporary usernames are generated using go template
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target-name` (optional, string): Target name
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `user-ttl` (optional, string, default: 60m): User TTL

## Creates AWS dynamic secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /dynamic-secret-create-aws._

`POST /gateway-create-producer-aws`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewaycreateproduceraws

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Dynamic secret name
- `access-mode` (optional, string)
- `admin-rotation-interval-days` (optional, integer, default: 0): Admin credentials rotation interval (days)
- `aws-access-key-id` (optional, string): Access Key ID
- `aws-access-secret-key` (optional, string): Secret Access Key
- `aws-external-id` (optional, string): The AWS External ID associated with the AWS role (relevant only for assume_role mode)
- `aws-role-arns` (optional, string): AWS Role ARNs to be used in the Assume Role operation (relevant only for assume_role mode)
- `aws-user-console-access` (optional, boolean, default: False): AWS User console access
- `aws-user-groups` (optional, string): AWS User groups
- `aws-user-policies` (optional, string): AWS User policies
- `aws-user-programmatic-access` (optional, boolean, default: True): Enable AWS User programmatic access
- `custom-username-template` (optional, string): Customize how temporary usernames are generated using go template
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `enable-admin-rotation` (optional, boolean, default: False): Automatic admin credentials rotation
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `password-length` (optional, string): The length of the password to be generated
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key
- `region` (optional, string, default: us-east-2): Region
- `secure-access-aws-account-id` (optional, string): The AWS account id
- `secure-access-aws-native-cli` (optional, boolean): The AWS native cli
- `secure-access-bastion-issuer` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-certificate-issuer
- `secure-access-certificate-issuer` (optional, string): Path to the SSH Certificate Issuer for your Akeyless Secure Access
- `secure-access-delay` (optional, integer): The delay duration, in seconds, to wait after generating just-in-time credentials. Accepted range: 0-120 seconds
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `secure-access-web` (optional, boolean, default: True): Enable Web Secure Remote Access
- `secure-access-web-browsing` (optional, boolean, default: False): Secure browser via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `secure-access-web-proxy` (optional, boolean, default: False): Web-Proxy via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `session-tags` (optional, string): String of Key value session tags comma separated, relevant only for Assumed Role
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target-name` (optional, string): Target name
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `transitive-tag-keys` (optional, string): String of transitive tag keys space separated, relevant only for Assumed Role
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-capital-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one uppercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (A to Z). [true/false]
- `use-lower-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one lowercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (a to z). [true/false]
- `use-numbers` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one numeric character (0 to 9). [true/false]
- `use-special-characters` (optional, string)
- `user-ttl` (optional, string, default: 60m): User TTL

## Creates Azure AD dynamic secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /dynamic-secret-create-azure._

`POST /gateway-create-producer-azure`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewaycreateproducerazure

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Dynamic secret name
- `app-obj-id` (optional, string): Azure App Object Id
- `azure-administrative-unit` (optional, string): Azure AD administrative unit (relevant only when azure-user-portal-access=true)
- `azure-client-id` (optional, string): Azure Client ID
- `azure-client-secret` (optional, string): Azure Client Secret
- `azure-tenant-id` (optional, string): Azure Tenant ID
- `custom-username-template` (optional, string): Customize how temporary usernames are generated using go template
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `fixed-user-claim-keyname` (optional, string, default: false): FixedUserClaimKeyname
- `fixed-user-only` (optional, boolean, default: False): Fixed user
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `password-length` (optional, string): The length of the password to be generated
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic secret encryption key
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `secure-access-url` (optional, string): Destination URL to inject secrets
- `secure-access-web` (optional, boolean, default: True): Enable Web Secure Remote Access
- `secure-access-web-browsing` (optional, boolean, default: False): Secure browser via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `secure-access-web-proxy` (optional, boolean, default: False): Web-Proxy via Akeyless's Secure Remote Access (SRA)
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target-name` (optional, string): Target name
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key
- `secure-access-allow-port-forwading` (optional, boolean): Enable Port forwarding while using CLI access
- `secure-access-bastion-issuer` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-certificate-issuer

- `secure-access-dashboard-url` (optional, string): The K8s dashboard url

## Creates Ping Federate dynamic secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /dynamic-secret-create-ping._

`POST /gateway-create-producer-ping`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewaycreateproducerping

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Dynamic secret name
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `ping-administrative-port` (optional, string, default: 9999): Ping Federate administrative port
- `ping-atm-id` (optional, string): Set a specific Access Token Management (ATM) instance for the created OAuth Client by providing the ATM Id. If no explicit value is given, the default pingfederate server ATM will be set.
- `ping-authorization-port` (optional, string, default: 9031): Ping Federate authorization port
- `ping-cert-subject-dn` (optional, string): The subject DN of the client certificate. If no explicit value is given, the producer will create CA certificate and matched client certificate and return it as value. Used in conjunction with ping-issuer-dn (relevant for CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE authentication method)
- `ping-client-authentication-type` (optional, string, default: CLIENT_SECRET): OAuth Client Authentication Type [CLIENT_SECRET, PRIVATE_KEY_JWT, CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE]
- `ping-enforce-replay-prevention` (optional, string, default: false): Determines whether PingFederate requires a unique signed JWT from the client for each action (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method) [true/false]
- `ping-grant-types` (optional, array): List of OAuth client grant types [IMPLICIT, AUTHORIZATION_CODE, CLIENT_CREDENTIALS, TOKEN_EXCHANGE, REFRESH_TOKEN, ASSERTION_GRANTS, PASSWORD, RESOURCE_OWNER_CREDENTIALS]. If no explicit value is given, AUTHORIZATION_CODE will be selected as default.
- `ping-issuer-dn` (optional, string): Issuer DN of trusted CA certificate that imported into Ping Federate server. You may select \"Trust Any\" to trust all the existing issuers in Ping Federate server. Used in conjunction with ping-cert-subject-dn (relevant for CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE authentication method)
- `ping-jwks` (optional, string): Base64-encoded JSON Web Key Set (JWKS). If no explicit value is given, the producer will create JWKs and matched signed JWT (Sign Algo: RS256) and return it as value (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-jwks-url` (optional, string): The URL of the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS). If no explicit value is given, the producer will create JWKs and matched signed JWT and return it as value (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-password` (optional, string): Ping Federate privileged user password
- `ping-privileged-user` (optional, string): Ping Federate privileged user
- `ping-redirect-uris` (optional, array): List of URIs to which the OAuth authorization server may redirect the resource owner's user agent after authorization is obtained. At least one redirection URI is required for the AUTHORIZATION_CODE and IMPLICIT grant types.
- `ping-restricted-scopes` (optional, array): Limit the OAuth client to specific scopes list
- `ping-signing-algo` (optional, string): The signing algorithm that the client must use to sign its request objects [RS256,RS384,RS512,ES256,ES384,ES512,PS256,PS384,PS512] If no explicit value is given, the client can use any of the supported signing algorithms (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-url` (optional, string): Ping URL
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target-name` (optional, string): Target name
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `user-ttl` (optional, string, default: 60m): The time from dynamic secret creation to expiration.

## Update Allowed Access

Available permissions: [defaults,targets,classic_keys,automatic_migration,ldap_auth,dynamic_secret,k8s_auth,log_forwarding,zero_knowledge_encryption,rotated_secret,caching,event_forwarding,admin,kmip,general,rotate_secret_value]

## Updates cache settings
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /gateway-update-cache`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewayupdatecache

Required: none

Fields:
- `backup-interval` (optional, string, default: 1): Secure backup interval in minutes. To ensure service continuity in case of power cycle and network outage secrets will be backed up periodically per backup interval
- `enable-cache` (optional, string): Enable cache [true/false]
- `enable-proactive` (optional, string): Enable proactive caching [true/false]
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `minimum-fetch-interval` (optional, string, default: 5): When using Cache or/and Proactive Cache, additional secrets will be fetched upon requesting a secret, based on the requestor's access policy. Define minimum fetching interval to avoid over fetching in a given time frame
- `stale-timeout` (optional, string, default: 60): Stale timeout in minutes, cache entries which are not accessed within timeout will be removed from cache
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Updates gateway item
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /gateway-update-item`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewayupdateitem

Required: name, type

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Item name
- `type` (**required**, string): Item type
- `add-tag` (optional, array): List of the new tags that will be attached to this item
- `api-id` (optional, string): API ID to rotate (relevant only for rotator-type=api-key)
- `api-key` (optional, string): API key to rotate (relevant only for rotator-type=api-key)
- `app-id` (optional, string): ApplicationId (used in azure)
- `auto-rotate` (optional, string): Whether to automatically rotate every --rotation-interval days, or disable existing automatic rotation [true/false]
- `custom-payload` (optional, string): Secret payload to be sent with rotation request (relevant only for rotator-type=custom)
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string, default: default_metadata): Description of the object
- `gcp-key` (optional, string): Base64-encoded service account private key text
- `gcp-service-account-email` (optional, string): The email of the gcp service account to rotate
- `gcp-service-account-key-id` (optional, string): The key id of the gcp service account to rotate
- `grace-rotation` (optional, string): Create a new access key without deleting the old key from AWS for backup (relevant only for AWS) [true/false]
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `keep-prev-version` (optional, string): Whether to keep previous version [true/false]. (relevant only for --type=rotated-secret). If not set, use default according to account settings
- `key` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `new-metadata` (optional, string, default: default_metadata): Deprecated - use description
- `new-name` (optional, string): New item name
- `new-version` (optional, boolean): Deprecated
- `password-length` (optional, string): The length of the password to be generated
- `rm-tag` (optional, array): List of the existent tags that will be removed from this item
- `rotated-password` (optional, string): rotated-username password (relevant only for rotator-type=password)
- `rotated-username` (optional, string): username to be rotated, if selected \"use-self-creds\" at rotator-creds-type, this username will try to rotate it's own password, if \"use-target-creds\" is selected, target credentials will be use to rotate the rotated-password (relevant only for rotator-type=password)
- `rotation-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the rotation of the item would you like to be notified
- `rotation-hour` (optional, integer, default: 0): The Rotation Hour
- `rotation-interval` (optional, string): The number of days to wait between every automatic key rotation (1-365)
- `rotator-creds-type` (optional, string, default: use-self-creds): The rotation credentials type
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Updates K8S Auth config
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /gateway-update-k8s-auth-config`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewayupdatek8sauthconfig

Required: access-id, k8s-host, name, new-name, signing-key

Fields:
- `access-id` (**required**, string): The access ID of the Kubernetes auth method
- `k8s-host` (**required**, string): The URL of the kubernetes API server
- `name` (**required**, string): K8S Auth config name
- `new-name` (**required**, string): K8S Auth config new name
- `signing-key` (**required**, string): The private key (base64 encoded) associated with the public key defined in the Kubernetes auth
- `cluster-api-type` (optional, string, default: native_k8s): Cluster access type. options: [native_k8s, rancher]
- `disable-issuer-validation` (optional, string): Disable issuer validation [true/false]
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `k8s-auth-type` (optional, string, default: token): K8S auth type [token/certificate]. (relevant for "native_k8s" only)
- `k8s-ca-cert` (optional, string): The CA Certificate (base64 encoded) to use to call into the kubernetes API server
- `k8s-client-certificate` (optional, string): Content of the k8 client certificate (PEM format) in a Base64 format (relevant for "native_k8s" only)
- `k8s-client-key` (optional, string): Content of the k8 client private key (PEM format) in a Base64 format (relevant for "native_k8s" only)
- `k8s-issuer` (optional, string, default: kubernetes/serviceaccount): The Kubernetes JWT issuer name. K8SIssuer is the claim that specifies who issued the Kubernetes token
- `rancher-api-key` (optional, string): The api key used to access the TokenReview API to validate other JWTs (relevant for "rancher" only)
- `rancher-cluster-id` (optional, string): The cluster id as define in rancher (relevant for "rancher" only)
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `token-exp` (optional, integer, default: 300): Time in seconds of expiration of the Akeyless Kube Auth Method token
- `token-reviewer-jwt` (optional, string): A Kubernetes service account JWT used to access the TokenReview API to validate other JWTs (relevant for "native_k8s" only). If not set, the JWT submitted in the authentication process will be used to access the Kubernetes TokenReview API.
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `use-gw-service-account` (optional, boolean): Use the GW's service account

## Update migration
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /gateway-update-migration`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewayupdatemigration

Required: hosts, si-target-name, si-users-path-template, target-location

Fields:
- `hosts` (**required**, string): A comma separated list of IPs, CIDR ranges, or DNS names to scan
- `si-target-name` (**required**, string): SSH, Windows or Linked Target Name. (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-users-path-template` (**required**, string): Path location template for migrating users as Rotated Secrets e.g.: .../Users/{{COMPUTER_NAME}}/{{USERNAME}} (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `target-location` (**required**, string): Target location in Akeyless for imported secrets
- `ServiceAccountKeyDecoded` (optional, string)
- `ad-auto-rotate` (optional, string): Enable/Disable automatic/recurrent rotation for migrated secrets. Default is false: only manual rotation is allowed for migrated secrets. If set to true, this command should be combined with --ad-rotation-interval and --ad-rotation-hour parameters (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-cert-expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of discovered certificates would you like to be notified (Relevant only for Active Directory migration with certificate discovery enabled)
- `ad-certificates-path-template` (optional, string): Path location template for migrating certificates e.g.: /Certificates/{{COMMON_NAME}} (Relevant only for Active Directory migration with certificate discovery enabled)
- `ad-computer-base-dn` (optional, string): Distinguished Name of Computer objects (servers) to search in Active Directory e.g.: CN=Computers,DC=example,DC=com (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-discover-iis-app` (optional, string, default: false): Enable/Disable discovery of IIS application from each domain server as part of the SSH/Windows Rotated Secrets. Default is false. (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-discover-services` (optional, string, default: false): Enable/Disable discovery of Windows services from each domain server as part of the SSH/Windows Rotated Secrets. Default is false. (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-discovery-types` (optional, array): Set migration discovery types (domain-users, computers, local-users). (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-domain-name` (optional, string): Active Directory Domain Name (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-domain-users-path-template` (optional, string): Path location template for migrating domain users as Rotated Secrets e.g.: .../DomainUsers/{{USERNAME}} (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-local-users-ignore` (optional, string): Comma-separated list of Local Users which should not be migrated (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-local-users-path-template` (optional, string): Path location template for migrating domain users as Rotated Secrets e.g.: .../LocalUsers/{{COMPUTER_NAME}}/{{USERNAME}} (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-os-filter` (optional, string): Filter by Operating System to run the migration, can be used with wildcards, e.g. SRV20* (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-rotation-hour` (optional, integer): The hour of the scheduled rotation in UTC (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-rotation-interval` (optional, integer): The number of days to wait between every automatic rotation [1-365] (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-sra-enable-rdp` (optional, string): Enable/Disable RDP Secure Remote Access for the migrated local users rotated secrets. Default is false: rotated secrets will not be created with SRA (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-ssh-port` (optional, string, default: 22): Set the SSH Port for further connection to the domain servers. Default is port 22 (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-target-format` (optional, string, default: linked): Relevant only for ad-discovery-types=computers. For linked, all computers will be migrated into a linked target(s). if set with regular, the migration will create a target for each computer.
- `ad-target-name` (optional, string): Active Directory LDAP Target Name. Server type should be Active Directory (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-targets-path-template` (optional, string): Path location template for migrating domain servers as SSH/Windows Targets e.g.: .../Servers/{{COMPUTER_NAME}} (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-targets-type` (optional, string, default: windows): Set the target type of the domain servers [ssh/windows](Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-user-base-dn` (optional, string): Distinguished Name of User objects to search in Active Directory, e.g.: CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-user-groups` (optional, string): Comma-separated list of domain groups from which privileged domain users will be migrated. If empty, migrate all users based on the --ad-user-base-dn (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad-winrm-over-http` (optional, string, default: false): Use WinRM over HTTP, by default runs over HTTPS
- `ad-winrm-port` (optional, string, default: 5986): Set the WinRM Port for further connection to the domain servers. Default is 5986 (Relevant only for Active Directory migration)
- `ad_discover_local_users` (optional, string): Enable/Disable discovery of local users from each domain server and migrate them as SSH/Windows Rotated Secrets. Default is false: only domain users will be migrated. Discovery of local users might require further installation of SSH on the servers, based on the supplied computer base DN. This will be implemented automatically as part of the migration process (Relevant only for Active Directory migration) Deprecated: use AdDiscoverTypes
- `ai-certificate-discovery` (optional, string): Enable AI-assisted certificate discovery (only when AI Insight is enabled on the Gateway)
- `aws-key` (optional, string): AWS Secret Access Key (relevant only for AWS migration)
- `aws-key-id` (optional, string): AWS Access Key ID with sufficient permissions to get all secrets, e.g. 'arn:aws:secretsmanager:[Region]:[AccountId]:secret:[/path/to/secrets/*]' (relevant only for AWS migration)
- `aws-region` (optional, string, default: us-east-2): AWS region of the required Secrets Manager (relevant only for AWS migration)
- `azure-client-id` (optional, string): Azure Key Vault Access client ID, should be Azure AD App with a service principal (relevant only for Azure Key Vault migration)
- `azure-kv-name` (optional, string): Azure Key Vault Name (relevant only for Azure Key Vault migration)
- `azure-secret` (optional, string): Azure Key Vault secret (relevant only for Azure Key Vault migration)
- `azure-tenant-id` (optional, string): Azure Key Vault Access tenant ID (relevant only for Azure Key Vault migration)
- `conjur-account` (optional, string): Conjur account name set on your Conjur server (relevant only for Conjur migration).
- `conjur-api-key` (optional, string): Conjur API Key for the specified user (relevant only for Conjur migration).
- `conjur-url` (optional, string): Conjur server base URL (relevant only for Conjur migration). If conjur-url is HTTPS and Conjur uses a private CA/self-signed certificate, make the CA bundle available on the Gateway and set CONJUR_SSL_CERT_PATH to its path.
- `conjur-username` (optional, string): Conjur username used to authenticate (relevant only for Conjur migration).
- `delete-remote` (optional, boolean): Delete the secret from the remote target as well, relevant only when usc-name is not empty (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration)
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the certificate would you like to be notified.
- `gcp-key` (optional, string): Base64-encoded GCP Service Account private key text with sufficient permissions to Secrets Manager, Minimum required permission is Secret Manager Secret Accessor, e.g. 'roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor' (relevant only for GCP migration)
- `gcp-project-id` (optional, string): GCP Project ID (cross-project override)
- `hashi-json` (optional, string, default: true): Import secret key as json value or independent secrets (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration) [true/false]
- `hashi-metadata-mode` (optional, string): Controls the amount of HashiCorp Vault secret metadata migrated with each secret value. Options: none|minimal|full
- `hashi-ns` (optional, array): HashiCorp Vault Namespaces is a comma-separated list of namespaces which need to be imported into Akeyless Vault. For every provided namespace, all its child namespaces are imported as well, e.g. nmsp/subnmsp1/subnmsp2,nmsp/anothernmsp. By default, import all namespaces (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration)
- `hashi-token` (optional, string): HashiCorp Vault access token with sufficient permissions to preform list & read operations on secrets objects (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration)
- `hashi-url` (optional, string): HashiCorp Vault API URL, e.g. https://vault-mgr01:8200 (relevant only for HasiCorp Vault migration)
- `id` (optional, string): Migration ID (Can be retrieved with gateway-list-migration command)
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `k8s-ca-certificate` (optional, array): For Certificate Authentication method K8s Cluster CA certificate (relevant only for K8s migration with Certificate Authentication method)
- `k8s-client-certificate` (optional, array): K8s Client certificate with sufficient permission to list and get secrets in the namespace(s) you selected (relevant only for K8s migration with Certificate Authentication method)
- `k8s-client-key` (optional, array): K8s Client key (relevant only for K8s migration with Certificate Authentication method)
- `k8s-namespace` (optional, string): K8s Namespace, Use this field to import secrets from a particular namespace only. By default, the secrets are imported from all namespaces (relevant only for K8s migration)
- `k8s-password` (optional, string): K8s Client password (relevant only for K8s migration with Password Authentication method)
- `k8s-skip-system` (optional, boolean): K8s Skip Control Plane Secrets, This option allows to avoid importing secrets from system namespaces (relevant only for K8s migration)
- `k8s-token` (optional, string): For Token Authentication method K8s Bearer Token with sufficient permission to list and get secrets in the namespace(s) you selected (relevant only for K8s migration with Token Authentication method)
- `k8s-url` (optional, string): K8s API Server URL, e.g. https://k8s-api.mycompany.com:6443 (relevant only for K8s migration)
- `k8s-username` (optional, string): For Password Authentication method K8s Client username with sufficient permission to list and get secrets in the namespace(s) you selected (relevant only for K8s migration with Password Authentication method)
- `name` (optional, string): Migration name
- `new-name` (optional, string): New migration name
- `port-ranges` (optional, string, default: 443): A comma separated list of port ranges Examples: "80,443" or "80,443,8080-8090" or "443"
- `protection-key` (optional, string): The name of the key that protects the classic key value (if empty, the account default key will be used)
- `si-auto-rotate` (optional, string): Enable/Disable automatic/recurrent rotation for migrated secrets. Default is false: only manual rotation is allowed for migrated secrets. If set to true, this command should be combined with --si-rotation-interval and --si-rotation-hour parameters (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-rotation-hour` (optional, integer): The hour of the scheduled rotation in UTC (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-rotation-interval` (optional, integer): The number of days to wait between every automatic rotation [1-365] (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-sra-enable-rdp` (optional, string, default: false): Enable/Disable RDP Secure Remote Access for the migrated local users rotated secrets. Default is false: rotated secrets will not be created with SRA (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-user-groups` (optional, string): Comma-separated list of groups to migrate users from. If empty, all users from all groups will be migrated (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `si-users-ignore` (optional, string): Comma-separated list of Local Users which should not be migrated (Relevant only for Server Inventory migration)
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `usc-name` (optional, string): Universal Secret Connector name
- `use-gw-cloud-identity` (optional, boolean): Use the GW's Cloud IAM

## Updates Ping Federate dynamic secret
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI, via canonical command /dynamic-secret-update-ping._

`POST /gateway-update-producer-ping`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/gatewayupdateproducerping

Required: name

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): Dynamic secret name
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `input-rule` (optional, array): Agentic input rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Sanitize input) Mirrors commands.AgenticRulesParams — kept separate because ResourceDS cannot embed it (different package, different struct layout).
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `new-name` (optional, string): Dynamic secret New name
- `output-rule` (optional, array): Agentic output rule in name=...,rule=... format (e.g. name=rule1,rule=Mask secrets)
- `ping-administrative-port` (optional, string, default: 9999): Ping Federate administrative port
- `ping-atm-id` (optional, string): Set a specific Access Token Management (ATM) instance for the created OAuth Client by providing the ATM Id. If no explicit value is given, the default pingfederate server ATM will be set.
- `ping-authorization-port` (optional, string, default: 9031): Ping Federate authorization port
- `ping-cert-subject-dn` (optional, string): The subject DN of the client certificate. If no explicit value is given, the producer will create CA certificate and matched client certificate and return it as value. Used in conjunction with ping-issuer-dn (relevant for CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE authentication method)
- `ping-client-authentication-type` (optional, string, default: CLIENT_SECRET): OAuth Client Authentication Type [CLIENT_SECRET, PRIVATE_KEY_JWT, CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE]
- `ping-enforce-replay-prevention` (optional, string, default: false): Determines whether PingFederate requires a unique signed JWT from the client for each action (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method) [true/false]
- `ping-grant-types` (optional, array): List of OAuth client grant types [IMPLICIT, AUTHORIZATION_CODE, CLIENT_CREDENTIALS, TOKEN_EXCHANGE, REFRESH_TOKEN, ASSERTION_GRANTS, PASSWORD, RESOURCE_OWNER_CREDENTIALS]. If no explicit value is given, AUTHORIZATION_CODE will be selected as default.
- `ping-issuer-dn` (optional, string): Issuer DN of trusted CA certificate that imported into Ping Federate server. You may select \"Trust Any\" to trust all the existing issuers in Ping Federate server. Used in conjunction with ping-cert-subject-dn (relevant for CLIENT_TLS_CERTIFICATE authentication method)
- `ping-jwks` (optional, string): Base64-encoded JSON Web Key Set (JWKS). If no explicit value is given, the producer will create JWKs and matched signed JWT (Sign Algo: RS256) and return it as value (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-jwks-url` (optional, string): The URL of the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS). If no explicit value is given, the producer will create JWKs and matched signed JWT and return it as value (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-password` (optional, string): Ping Federate privileged user password
- `ping-privileged-user` (optional, string): Ping Federate privileged user
- `ping-redirect-uris` (optional, array): List of URIs to which the OAuth authorization server may redirect the resource owner's user agent after authorization is obtained. At least one redirection URI is required for the AUTHORIZATION_CODE and IMPLICIT grant types.
- `ping-restricted-scopes` (optional, array): Limit the OAuth client to specific scopes list
- `ping-signing-algo` (optional, string): The signing algorithm that the client must use to sign its request objects [RS256,RS384,RS512,ES256,ES384,ES512,PS256,PS384,PS512] If no explicit value is given, the client can use any of the supported signing algorithms (relevant for PRIVATE_KEY_JWT authentication method)
- `ping-url` (optional, string): Ping URL
- `producer-encryption-key-name` (optional, string): Dynamic producer encryption key
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `target-name` (optional, string): Target name
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `user-ttl` (optional, string, default: 60m): The time from dynamic secret creation to expiration.

## Get credentials for authentication with Kubernetes cluster based on a PKI Cert Issuer
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /get-kube-exec-creds`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/getkubeexeccreds

Required: cert-issuer-name

Fields:
- `cert-issuer-name` (**required**, string): The name of the PKI certificate issuer
- `alt-names` (optional, string): The Subject Alternative Names to be included in the PKI certificate (in a comma-separated list) (if CSR is supplied this flag is ignored and any DNS.* names are taken from it)
- `api-version` (optional, string, default: v1): Client authentication API version
- `common-name` (optional, string): The common name to be included in the PKI certificate (if CSR is supplied this flag is ignored and the CSR subject CN is taken)

- `extra-extensions` (optional, string): A json string that defines the requested extra extensions for the certificate
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key-data-base64` (optional, string): PKI key file contents. If this option is used, the certificate will be printed to stdout
- `max-path-len` (optional, integer, default: -1): The maximum path length for the generated certificate. -1, means unlimited unless the signing certificate has a maximum path length set

## Creates a new PKI certificate issuer
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /create-pki-cert-issuer`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createpkicertissuer

Required: name, ttl

Fields:
- `name` (**required**, string): PKI certificate issuer name
- `ttl` (**required**, string): The maximum requested Time To Live for issued certificates, in seconds. In case of Public CA, this is based on the CA target's supported maximum TTLs
- `allow-any-name` (optional, boolean): If set, clients can request certificates for any CN
- `allow-copy-ext-from-csr` (optional, boolean): If set, will allow copying the extra extensions from the csr file (if given)
- `allow-subdomains` (optional, boolean): If set, clients can request certificates for subdomains of the allowed domains
- `allowed-domains` (optional, string): A list of the allowed domains that clients can request to be included in the certificate (in a comma-delimited list)
- `allowed-extra-extensions` (optional, string): A json string containing the allowed extra extensions for the pki cert issuer
- `allowed-ip-sans` (optional, string): A list of the allowed CIDRs for ips that clients can request to be included in the certificate as part of the IP Subject Alternative Names (in a comma-delimited list)
- `allowed-uri-sans` (optional, string): A list of the allowed URIs that clients can request to be included in the certificate as part of the URI Subject Alternative Names (in a comma-delimited list)
- `auto-renew` (optional, boolean): Automatically renew certificates before expiration
- `basic-constraints` (optional, string): Defines the X.509 Basic Constraints extension for certificates issued by this PKI issuer template
- `ca-target` (optional, string): The name of an existing CA target to attach this PKI Certificate Issuer to, required in Public CA mode
- `client-flag` (optional, boolean): If set, certificates will be flagged for client auth use
- `code-signing-flag` (optional, boolean): If set, certificates will be flagged for code signing use
- `country` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of countries that will be set in the issued certificate
- `create-private-crl` (optional, boolean): Set this to allow the issuer will expose a CRL endpoint in the Gateway
- `create-private-ocsp` (optional, boolean): Set this to enable an OCSP endpoint in the Gateway and include its URL in AIA
- `create-public-crl` (optional, boolean): Set this to allow the cert issuer will expose a public CRL endpoint
- `create-public-ocsp` (optional, boolean): Set this to enable a public OCSP endpoint and include its URL in AIA (served by UAM and includes account id)
- `critical-key-usage` (optional, string, default: true): Mark key usage as critical [true/false]
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `destination-path` (optional, string): A path in which to save generated certificates
- `disable-wildcards` (optional, boolean): If set, generation of wildcard certificates will be disabled.
- `enable-acme` (optional, boolean): If set, the cert issuer will support the acme protocol
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the certificate would you like to be notified.
- `gw-cluster-url` (optional, string): The GW cluster URL to issue the certificate from. Required in Public CA mode, to allow CRLs on private CA, or to enable ACME
- `is-ca` (optional, boolean): If set, the basic constraints extension will be added to certificate
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key-usage` (optional, string, default: DigitalSignature,KeyAgreement,KeyEncipherment): key-usage
- `locality` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of localities that will be set in the issued certificate
- `max-path-len` (optional, integer, default: -1): The maximum path length for the generated certificate. -1, means unlimited
- `metadata` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `not-enforce-hostnames` (optional, boolean): If set, any names are allowed for CN and SANs in the certificate and not only a valid host name
- `not-require-cn` (optional, boolean): If set, clients can request certificates without a CN
- `ocsp-ttl` (optional, string): OCSP NextUpdate window for OCSP responses (min 10m). Supports s,m,h,d suffix.
- `organizational-units` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of organizational units (OU) that will be set in the issued certificate
- `organizations` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of organizations (O) that will be set in the issued certificate
- `postal-code` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of postal codes that will be set in the issued certificate
- `protect-certificates` (optional, boolean): Whether to protect generated certificates from deletion
- `province` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of provinces that will be set in the issued certificate
- `scheduled-renew` (optional, integer): Number of days before expiration to renew certificates
- `server-flag` (optional, boolean): If set, certificates will be flagged for server auth use
- `signer-key-name` (optional, string): A key to sign the certificate with, required in Private CA mode
- `street-address` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of street addresses that will be set in the issued certificate
- `tag` (optional, array): List of the tags attached to this key
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Creates a new SSH certificate issuer
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /create-ssh-cert-issuer`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createsshcertissuer

Required: allowed-users, name, signer-key-name, ttl

Fields:
- `allowed-users` (**required**, string, default: -): Users allowed to fetch the certificate, e.g root,ubuntu
- `name` (**required**, string): SSH certificate issuer name
- `signer-key-name` (**required**, string): A key to sign the certificate with
- `ttl` (**required**, integer): The requested Time To Live for the certificate, in seconds
- `ProviderType` (optional, string)
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `extensions` (optional, object): Signed certificates with extensions, e.g permit-port-forwarding=\"\"
- `external-username` (optional, string, default: false): Externally provided username [true/false]
- `fixed-user-claim-keyname` (optional, string): For externally provided users, denotes the key-name of IdP claim to extract the username from (relevant only for external-username=true)
- `host-provider` (optional, string): Host provider type [explicit/target], Default Host provider is explicit, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access of ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `metadata` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `principals` (optional, string): Signed certificates with principal, e.g example_role1,example_role2
- `secure-access-api` (optional, string): Secure Access SSH control API endpoint. E.g. https://my.sra-server:9900
- `secure-access-bastion-api` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-api
- `secure-access-bastion-ssh` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-ssh
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `secure-access-enforce-hosts-restriction` (optional, boolean): Enable this flag to enforce connections only to the hosts listed in --secure-access-host
- `secure-access-gateway` (optional, string)
- `secure-access-host` (optional, array): Target servers for connections (In case of Linked Target association, host(s) will inherit Linked Target hosts - Relevant only for Dynamic Secrets/producers)
- `secure-access-ssh` (optional, string): Bastion's SSH server. E.g. my.sra-server:22
- `secure-access-ssh-creds-user` (optional, string): SSH username to connect to target server, must be in 'Allowed Users' list
- `secure-access-use-internal-bastion` (optional, boolean): Deprecated. Use secure-access-use-internal-ssh-access
- `secure-access-use-internal-ssh-access` (optional, boolean): Use internal SSH Access
- `tag` (optional, array): List of the tags attached to this key
- `target` (optional, array): A list of linked targets to be associated, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access for ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret, To specify multiple targets use argument multiple times
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
