- `basic-constraints` (optional, string): Defines the X.509 Basic Constraints extension for certificates issued by this PKI issuer template
- `client-flag` (optional, boolean): If set, certificates will be flagged for client auth use
- `code-signing-flag` (optional, boolean): If set, certificates will be flagged for code signing use
- `country` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of countries that will be set in the issued certificate
- `create-private-crl` (optional, boolean): Set this to allow the issuer will expose a CRL endpoint in the Gateway
- `create-private-ocsp` (optional, boolean): Set this to enable an OCSP endpoint in the Gateway and include its URL in AIA
- `create-public-crl` (optional, boolean): Set this to allow the cert issuer will expose a public CRL endpoint
- `create-public-ocsp` (optional, boolean): Set this to enable a public OCSP endpoint and include its URL in AIA (served by UAM and includes account id)
- `critical-key-usage` (optional, string, default: true): Mark key usage as critical [true/false]
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `destination-path` (optional, string): A path in which to save generated certificates
- `disable-wildcards` (optional, boolean): If set, generation of wildcard certificates will be disabled.
- `enable-acme` (optional, boolean): If set, the cert issuer will support the acme protocol
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the certificate would you like to be notified.
- `gw-cluster-url` (optional, string): The GW cluster URL to issue the certificate from. Required in Public CA mode, to allow CRLs on private CA, or to enable ACME
- `is-ca` (optional, boolean): If set, the basic constraints extension will be added to certificate
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key-usage` (optional, string, default: DigitalSignature,KeyAgreement,KeyEncipherment): key-usage
- `locality` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of localities that will be set in the issued certificate
- `max-path-len` (optional, integer, default: -1): The maximum path length for the generated certificate. -1, means unlimited
- `metadata` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `new-name` (optional, string): New item name
- `not-enforce-hostnames` (optional, boolean): If set, any names are allowed for CN and SANs in the certificate and not only a valid host name
- `not-require-cn` (optional, boolean): If set, clients can request certificates without a CN
- `ocsp-ttl` (optional, string): OCSP NextUpdate window for OCSP responses (min 10m). Supports s,m,h,d suffix.
- `organizational-units` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of organizational units (OU) that will be set in the issued certificate
- `organizations` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of organizations (O) that will be set in the issued certificate
- `postal-code` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of postal codes that will be set in the issued certificate
- `protect-certificates` (optional, boolean): Whether to protect generated certificates from deletion
- `province` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of provinces that will be set in the issued certificate
- `rm-tag` (optional, array): List of the existent tags that will be removed from this item
- `scheduled-renew` (optional, integer): Number of days before expiration to renew certificates
- `server-flag` (optional, boolean): If set, certificates will be flagged for server auth use
- `signer-key-name` (optional, string): A key to sign the certificate with, required in Private CA mode
- `street-address` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of street addresses that will be set in the issued certificate
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Updates a new SSH certificate issuer
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /update-ssh-cert-issuer`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/updatesshcertissuer

Required: allowed-users, name, signer-key-name, ttl

Fields:
- `allowed-users` (**required**, string, default: -): Users allowed to fetch the certificate, e.g root,ubuntu
- `name` (**required**, string): SSH certificate issuer name
- `signer-key-name` (**required**, string): A key to sign the certificate with
- `ttl` (**required**, integer): The requested Time To Live for the certificate, in seconds
- `ProviderType` (optional, string)
- `add-tag` (optional, array): List of the new tags that will be attached to this item
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `extensions` (optional, object): Signed certificates with extensions, e.g permit-port-forwarding=\"\"
- `external-username` (optional, string, default: false): Externally provided username [true/false]
- `fixed-user-claim-keyname` (optional, string): For externally provided users, denotes the key-name of IdP claim to extract the username from (relevant only for external-username=true)
- `host-provider` (optional, string): Host provider type [explicit/target], Default Host provider is explicit, Relevant only for Secure Remote Access of ssh cert issuer, ldap rotated secret and ldap dynamic secret
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `metadata` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `new-name` (optional, string): New item name
- `principals` (optional, string): Signed certificates with principal, e.g example_role1,example_role2
- `rm-tag` (optional, array): List of the existent tags that will be removed from this item
- `secure-access-api` (optional, string): Secure Access SSH control API endpoint. E.g. https://my.sra-server:9900
- `secure-access-bastion-api` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-api
- `secure-access-bastion-ssh` (optional, string): Deprecated. use secure-access-ssh
- `secure-access-enable` (optional, string): Enable/Disable secure remote access [true/false]
- `secure-access-enforce-hosts-restriction` (optional, boolean): Enable this flag to enforce connections only to the hosts listed in --secure-access-host
- `secure-access-gateway` (optional, string)
- `secure-access-host` (optional, array): Target servers for connections (In case of Linked Target association, host(s) will inherit Linked Target hosts - Relevant only for Dynamic Secrets/producers)
- `secure-access-ssh` (optional, string): Bastion's SSH server. E.g. my.sra-server:22
- `secure-access-ssh-creds-user` (optional, string): SSH username to connect to target server, must be in 'Allowed Users' list
- `secure-access-use-internal-bastion` (optional, boolean): Deprecated. Use secure-access-use-internal-ssh-access
- `secure-access-use-internal-ssh-access` (optional, boolean): Use internal SSH Access
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Create a new secret within the Universal Secrets Connector
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /usc-create`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/usccreate

Required: secret-name, usc-name, value

Fields:
- `secret-name` (**required**, string): Name for the new universal secrets
- `usc-name` (**required**, string): Name of the Universal Secrets Connector item
- `value` (**required**, string): Value of the universal secrets item, either text or base64 encoded binary
- `binary-value` (optional, boolean): Use this option if the universal secrets value is a base64 encoded binary
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the universal secrets
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `namespace` (optional, string): The namespace (relevant for Hashi vault target)
- `object-type` (optional, string)
- `pfx-password` (optional, string): Optional, the passphrase that protects the private key within the pfx certificate (Relevant only for Azure KV certificates)
- `region` (optional, string): Optional, create secret in a specific region (GCP only). If empty, a global secret will be created (provider default).
- `remote-secret-activation-date` (optional, string): Activation date for the secret on the remote endpoint, in UTC format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
- `remote-secret-expires` (optional, string): Expiration time for the secret on the remote endpoint, in UTC format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
- `selected-repositories` (optional, string)
- `tags` (optional, object): Tags for the universal secrets
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `usc-encryption-key` (optional, string): Optional, The name of the remote key that used to encrypt the secret value (if empty, the default key will be used)

## Configure client profile.
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /configure`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/configure

Required: none

Fields:
- `access-id` (optional, string): Access ID
- `access-key` (optional, string): Access Key
- `access-type` (optional, string, default: access_key): Access Type (access_key/password/azure_ad/saml/oidc/aws_iam/gcp/k8s/cert)
- `account-id` (optional, string): Account id (relevant only for access-type=password where the email address is associated with more than one account)
- `admin-email` (optional, string): Email (relevant only for access-type=password)
- `admin-password` (optional, string): Password (relevant only for access-type=password)
- `azure-ad-object-id` (optional, string): Azure Active Directory ObjectId (relevant only for access-type=azure_ad)
- `azure-cloud` (optional, string, default: AzureCloud): Azure cloud environment to use. Values: AzureCloud (default), AzureUSGovernment, AzureChinaCloud.
- `cert-data` (optional, string): Certificate data encoded in base64. Used if file was not provided. (relevant only for access-type=cert in Curl Context)
- `cert-issuer-name` (optional, string): Certificate Issuer Name
- `cert-username` (optional, string): The username to sign in the SSH certificate (use a comma-separated list for more than one username)
- `default-location-prefix` (optional, string): Default path prefix for name of items, targets and auth methods
- `disable-pafxfast` (optional, string): Disable the FAST negotiation in the Kerberos authentication method
- `gateway-spn` (optional, string): The service principal name of the gateway as registered in LDAP (i.e., HTTP/gateway)
- `gcp-audience` (optional, string, default: akeyless.io): GCP JWT audience
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `k8s-auth-config-name` (optional, string): The K8S Auth config name (relevant only for access-type=k8s)
- `kerberos-token` (optional, string): KerberosToken represents a Kerberos token generated for the gateway SPN (Service Principal Name).
- `kerberos-username` (optional, string): TThe username for the entry within the keytab to authenticate via Kerberos
- `key-data` (optional, string): Private key data encoded in base64. Used if file was not provided.(relevant only for access-type=cert in Curl Context)
- `keytab-data` (optional, string): Base64-encoded content of a valid keytab file, containing the service account's entry.
- `krb5-conf-data` (optional, string): Base64-encoded content of a valid krb5.conf file, specifying the settings and parameters required for Kerberos authentication.
- `legacy-signing-alg-name` (optional, boolean): Set this option to output legacy ('ssh-rsa-cert-v01@openssh.com') signing algorithm name in the certificate.
- `oci-auth-type` (optional, string, default: apikey): The type of the OCI configuration to use [instance/apikey/resource] (relevant only for access-type=oci)
- `oci-group-ocid` (optional, array): A list of Oracle Cloud IDs groups (relevant only for access-type=oci)

## Update account settings. The operation is allowed only for admin user
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /update-account-settings`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/updateaccountsettings

Required: none

Fields:
- `address` (optional, string): Address
- `allowed-client-type` (optional, array): A default list of client types that are allowed to authenticate [cli,ui,gateway-admin,sdk,mobile,extension].
- `allowed-email-domains` (optional, array): Limits email sharing to the specified domains. Relevant only when item sharing is enabled. By default, all domains are allowed.
- `bound-ips` (optional, array): A default list of comma-separated CIDR block that are allowed to authenticate.
- `city` (optional, string): City
- `company-name` (optional, string): Company name
- `country` (optional, string): Country
- `default-certificate-expiration-notification-days` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the certificate would you like to be notified. To specify multiple events, use argument multiple times: --default-certificate-expiration-notification-days 1 --default-certificate-expiration-notification-days 5
- `default-key-name` (optional, string): Set the account default key based on the DFC key name. Use "set-original-akeyless-default-key" to revert to using the original default key of the account.
- `default-share-link-ttl-minutes` (optional, string): Set the default ttl in minutes for sharing item number between 60 and 43200
- `default-versioning` (optional, string): If set to true, new versions is enabled by default
- `dp-enable-classic-key-protection` (optional, string): Set to update protection with classic keys state [true/false]
- `dynamic-secret-max-ttl` (optional, integer): Set the maximum ttl for dynamic secrets
- `dynamic-secret-max-ttl-enable` (optional, string): Set a maximum ttl for dynamic secrets [true/false]
- `enable-ai-insights` (optional, string): Enable AI insights [true/false]
- `enable-default-certificate-expiration-event` (optional, string): How many days before the expiration of the certificate would you like to be notified. [true/false]
- `enable-item-sharing` (optional, string): Enable sharing items [true/false]
- `enable-password-expiration` (optional, string): Enable password expiration policy [true/false]
- `force-new-versions` (optional, string): If set to true, new version will be created on update
- `gw-bound-ips` (optional, array): A default list of comma-separated CIDR block that acts as a trusted Gateway entity.
- `hide-personal-folder` (optional, string): Hide personal folder, if set - users will not be able to use personal folder [true/false]
- `hide-static-password` (optional, string): Hide static secret's password type [true/false]
- `invalid-characters` (optional, string, default: notReceivedInvalidCharacter): Characters that cannot be used for items/targets/roles/auths/event_forwarder names. Empty string will enforce nothing.
- `item-locking-enabled` (optional, string): Enable item locking feature [true/false]
- `item-type` (optional, string): VersionSettingsObjectType defines object types for account version settings
- `items-deletion-protection` (optional, string): Set or unset the default behaviour of items deletion protection [true/false]
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `jwt-ttl-default` (optional, integer): Default ttl
- `jwt-ttl-max` (optional, integer): Maximum ttl
- `jwt-ttl-min` (optional, integer): Minimum ttl
- `lock-allowed-client-type` (optional, string): Lock allowed-client-type setting in the account [true/false].
- `lock-bound-ips` (optional, string): Lock bound-ips setting globally in the account.
- `lock-default-key` (optional, string): Lock the account's default protection key, if set - users will not be able to use a different protection key, relevant only if default-key-name is configured [true/false]
- `lock-gw-bound-ips` (optional, string): Lock gw-bound-ips setting in the account.
- `lock-max-ttl` (optional, integer): Set the maximum TTL for item/target locks in minutes
- `max-rotation-interval` (optional, integer): Set the maximum rotation interval for rotated secrets auto rotation settings
- `max-rotation-interval-enable` (optional, string): Set a maximum rotation interval for rotated secrets auto rotation settings [true/false]
- `max-versions` (optional, string): Max versions
- `password-expiration-days` (optional, string): Specifies the number of days that a password is valid before it must be changed. A default value of 90 days is used.
- `password-expiration-notification-days` (optional, string): Specifies the number of days before a user receives notification that their password will expire. A default value of 14 days is used.
- `password-length` (optional, integer): Password length between 5 - to 50 characters
- `phone` (optional, string): Phone number
- `postal-code` (optional, string): Postal code
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `usage-event-enable` (optional, string): Enable event for objects that have not been used or changed [true/false]
- `usage-event-interval` (optional, integer): Interval by days for unused objects. Default and minimum interval is 90 days
- `usage-event-object-type` (optional, string): Usage event is supported for auth method or secrets-and-keys [auth/item]
- `use-capital-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one uppercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (A to Z). [true/false]
- `use-lower-letters` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one lowercase character from the ISO basic Latin alphabet (a to z). [true/false]
- `use-numbers` (optional, string): Specifies whether the generated temporary password must contain at least one numeric character (0 to 9). [true/false]
- `use-special-characters` (optional, string)

## Creates a new Classic Key
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /create-classic-key`  -  MUTATING (changes state)
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/createclassickey

Required: alg, name

Fields:
- `alg` (**required**, string): Classic Key type; options: [AES128GCM, AES256GCM, AES128SIV, AES256SIV, RSA1024, RSA2048, RSA3072, RSA4096, EC256, EC384, GPG]
- `name` (**required**, string): ClassicKey name
- `auto-rotate` (optional, string): Whether to automatically rotate every rotation_interval days, or disable existing automatic rotation [true/false]
- `cert-file-data` (optional, string): Certificate in a PEM format.
- `certificate-common-name` (optional, string): Common name for the generated certificate. Relevant only for generate-self-signed-certificate.
- `certificate-country` (optional, string): Country name for the generated certificate. Relevant only for generate-self-signed-certificate.
- `certificate-digest-algo` (optional, string): Digest algorithm to be used for the certificate key signing.
- `certificate-format` (optional, string)
- `certificate-locality` (optional, string): Locality for the generated certificate. Relevant only for generate-self-signed-certificate.
- `certificate-organization` (optional, string): Organization name for the generated certificate. Relevant only for generate-self-signed-certificate.
- `certificate-province` (optional, string): Province name for the generated certificate. Relevant only for generate-self-signed-certificate.
- `certificate-ttl` (optional, integer): TTL in days for the generated certificate. Required only for generate-self-signed-certificate.
- `conf-file-data` (optional, string): The csr config data in base64 encoding
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `description` (optional, string): Description of the object
- `expiration-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the expiration of the certificate would you like to be notified.
- `generate-self-signed-certificate` (optional, boolean): Whether to generate a self signed certificate with the key. If set, --certificate-ttl must be provided.
- `gpg-alg` (optional, string): gpg alg: Relevant only if GPG key type selected; options: [RSA1024, RSA2048, RSA3072, RSA4096, Ed25519]
- `hash-algorithm` (optional, string, default: SHA256): Specifies the hash algorithm used for the encryption key's operations, available options: [SHA256, SHA384, SHA512]
- `item-custom-fields` (optional, object): Additional custom fields to associate with the item
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key-data` (optional, string): Base64-encoded classic key value
- `metadata` (optional, string): Deprecated - use description
- `protection-key-name` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the secret value (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `rotation-event-in` (optional, array): How many days before the rotation of the item would you like to be notified
- `rotation-interval` (optional, string): The number of days to wait between every automatic rotation (1-365)
- `tags` (optional, array): Add tags attached to this object
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Creates a new PKI CA and Intermediate issuers
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /generate-ca`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/generateca

Required: allowed-domains, pki-chain-name, ttl

Fields:
- `allowed-domains` (**required**, string): A list of the allowed domains that clients can request to be included in the certificate (in a comma-delimited list)
- `pki-chain-name` (**required**, string): PKI chain name
- `ttl` (**required**, string): The maximum requested Time To Live for issued certificate by default in seconds, supported formats are s,m,h,d
- `alg` (optional, string)
- `delete_protection` (optional, string): Protection from accidental deletion of this object [true/false]
- `extended-key-usage` (optional, string, default: serverauth,clientauth): A comma sepereted list of extended key usage for the intermediate (serverauth / clientauth / codesigning)
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `key-type` (optional, string)
- `max-path-len` (optional, integer, default: 1): The maximum number of intermediate certificates that can appear in a certification path
- `protection-key-name` (optional, string): The name of a key that used to encrypt the secrets values (if empty, the account default protectionKey key will be used)
- `split-level` (optional, integer, default: 3): The number of fragments that the item will be split into
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication

## Generate a new CSR
_Command description from the Akeyless CLI._

`POST /generate-csr`  -  read-only
Reference: https://docs.akeyless.io/reference/generatecsr

Required: common-name, key-type, name

Fields:
- `common-name` (**required**, string): The common name to be included in the CSR certificate
- `key-type` (**required**, string, default: classic-key): The type of the key to generate (classic-key/dfc)
- `name` (**required**, string): The key name
- `alg` (optional, string)
- `alt-names` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of dns alternative names
- `certificate-type` (optional, string): The certificate type to be included in the CSR certificate (ssl-client/ssl-server/certificate-signing)
- `city` (optional, string): The city to be included in the CSR certificate
- `country` (optional, string): The country to be included in the CSR certificate
- `critical` (optional, boolean): Add critical to the key usage extension (will be false if not added)
- `dep` (optional, string): The department to be included in the CSR certificate
- `email-addresses` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of email addresses alternative names
- `export-private-key` (optional, boolean, default: False): The flag to indicate if the private key should be exported
- `generate-key` (optional, boolean): Generate a new classic key for the csr
- `hash-algorithm` (optional, string, default: SHA256): Specifies the hash algorithm used for the encryption key's operations, available options: SHA256, SHA384, SHA512
- `ip-addresses` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of ip addresses alternative names
- `json` (optional, boolean, default: False): Set output format to JSON
- `org` (optional, string): The organization to be included in the CSR certificate
- `split-level` (optional, integer, default: 3): The number of fragments that the item will be split into (not includes customer fragment)
- `state` (optional, string): The state to be included in the CSR certificate
- `token` (optional, string): Authentication token (see `/auth` and `/configure`)
- `uid-token` (optional, string): The universal identity token, Required only for universal_identity authentication
- `uri-sans` (optional, string): A comma-separated list of uri alternative names
